requirements.requirement: Designate a named accountable owner (minimum CISO or equivalent) with formal responsibility for the design, delivery, measurement, and continuous improvement of the cybersecurity awareness programme, with authority to enforce completion across all business lines.
requirements.rationale: The clause places the obligation on the financial institution as an entity, requiring a single accountable owner to prevent diffusion of responsibility. Absent clear ownership, programme gaps — such as missed annual cycles or outdated content — are not escalated or remediated.
requirements.frequency: Ownership confirmed annually in programme governance documentation

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal annual programme effectiveness review process that aggregates quantitative metrics (completion rates, phishing simulation results, assessment scores) and qualitative feedback, produces a management report, and presents findings with remediation recommendations to the Board Risk Committee.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the institution to 'measure the effectiveness' of its programme. A formal review cycle with board-level reporting ensures findings drive action rather than remain at operational level, and creates the audit trail BNM examiners will seek as evidence of active governance.
requirements.frequency: Annual formal review; interim updates to Risk Committee if effectiveness metrics breach defined thresholds

requirements.requirement: Embed cybersecurity awareness training obligations for third-party service providers into vendor contract templates, third-party risk assessments, and annual vendor review processes, with non-compliance treated as a contractual and risk management issue subject to escalation.
requirements.rationale: The clause cross-references paragraph 10.48(i) and requires third-party inclusion 'where appropriate'. Without contractual and governance anchors, third-party training obligations are unenforceable, creating an uncontrolled human-risk gap in the extended enterprise and a gap in BNM compliance evidence.
requirements.frequency: Embedded at contract initiation; reviewed at annual vendor risk assessment; verified at each annual training cycle

requirements.requirement: Implement a management attestation process requiring department heads to formally confirm annual training completion for their respective staff populations, with unresolved non-completion escalated to the CEO and reported in the institution's internal audit and risk reporting cycles.
requirements.rationale: The 'all staff' obligation requires accountability at the business unit level to ensure no population is inadvertently excluded. Attestation by senior managers creates accountability, distributes the compliance obligation beyond IT and HR, and provides BNM with evidence of institutionally enforced participation.
requirements.frequency: Annual, aligned with training cycle close; exceptions reported quarterly to the Board Risk Committee

business.summary: Financial institutions face significant operational and reputational risk when technology, cybersecurity, and risk management staff lack current competencies or industry-recognised certifications. Non-compliance exposes the institution to regulatory sanctions, heightened vulnerability to cyber incidents, and failures in technology risk controls. Sustained investment in staff training is therefore a business-critical obligation under RMiT, not a discretionary HR activity.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain an annual training budget specifically allocated to technology operations, cybersecurity, and risk management staff, covering both internal programmes and external certification costs.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates 'adequate' training, which implies sufficient resourcing. Without a dedicated, protected budget, training obligations are routinely deprioritised during cost-cutting cycles, creating compliance gaps and operational risk.
requirements.owner: Chief Financial Officer (CFO) in collaboration with Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO)
requirements.frequency: Annual budget cycle, reviewed quarterly against expenditure

requirements.requirement: Conduct a formal role mapping exercise to identify all staff whose functions fall within technology operations, cybersecurity, or risk management, and document their minimum competency and certification requirements by role.
requirements.rationale: The clause specifies training must enable staff to 'effectively perform their roles and responsibilities'. Without explicit role-to-requirement mapping, the institution cannot demonstrate adequacy of training coverage to BNM examiners.
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer (COO) in coordination with CISO and CRO
requirements.frequency: Annual review, updated upon organisational restructuring or role changes

requirements.requirement: Define minimum mandatory certification standards for each covered role category (e.g., CISSP or CISM for cybersecurity leads; CRISC or equivalent for risk management personnel; vendor-specific credentials for technology operations staff) and enforce these as conditions of role tenure.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly requires staff to be 'suitably certified'. Specifying and enforcing certification standards translates this obligation into a measurable, auditable business requirement rather than a subjective judgement.
requirements.owner: Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO) endorsed by Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
requirements.frequency: Standards reviewed annually; certification status tracked continuously

requirements.requirement: Incorporate training completion rates and certification attainment as formal Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in the performance appraisal framework for technology operations, cybersecurity, and risk management staff and their line managers.
requirements.rationale: Linking regulatory training obligations to individual performance accountability creates structural incentives for compliance. This directly supports the clause's requirement for 'continuous' training by making non-participation a documented performance issue.
requirements.frequency: Quarterly tracking; formal appraisal annually

requirements.requirement: Establish a succession and competency continuity plan to ensure that critical technology and cybersecurity roles are never left uncovered by staff who meet the minimum competency and certification thresholds defined under 15.2-B3.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires staff to be competent to 'effectively perform their roles and responsibilities', implying operational continuity. Staff attrition or role transitions without competency continuity planning creates immediate regulatory exposure.
requirements.frequency: Reviewed semi-annually and triggered upon any critical staff departure

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement platforms and processes that systematically deliver, track, and evidence continuous training and certification for all in-scope staff. This includes a Learning Management System (LMS) with role-based curriculum management, skills gap assessment tooling, and integration with HR systems to enforce training prerequisites and flag compliance deficiencies in real time. Evidence of completed training and valid certifications must be retained in auditable form for regulatory examination.

requirements.requirement: Deploy and configure a Learning Management System (LMS) that supports role-based training path assignment, automated enrolment, completion tracking, and certificate-of-completion generation for all technology operations, cybersecurity, and risk management staff.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires training to be 'adequate and continuous', which demands a systematic delivery mechanism. An LMS provides auditable evidence of training delivery, completion, and content relevance by role — evidence required during BNM supervisory examinations.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer (CIO) / Head of IT
requirements.frequency: System maintained continuously; training content reviewed and updated annually or when threat landscape changes materially

requirements.requirement: Implement an automated certification registry and expiry tracking system, integrated with the LMS and HR information system (HRIS), that generates alerts to staff and line managers at 90, 60, and 30 days prior to certification expiry, and flags lapsed certifications as a compliance risk in the institution's GRC platform.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires staff to be 'suitably certified' — implying certifications must remain current and valid. Manual tracking is error-prone at scale; automated expiry management ensures no certification lapses go undetected between audit cycles.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) in coordination with Head of IT
requirements.frequency: Real-time monitoring; monthly compliance status report generated automatically

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual technology-specific skills gap assessment using structured competency frameworks (e.g., NICE Cybersecurity Workforce Framework, SFIA) to identify training deficiencies at the individual and team level, and use outputs to update training plans for the following year.
requirements.rationale: Training must be 'adequate' relative to current roles. Without a structured skills gap assessment, training plans may not address actual competency shortfalls, particularly as technology risk evolves. This provides an evidence base for demonstrating adequacy to BNM.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and Head of Technology Risk
requirements.frequency: Annual assessment; results reviewed quarterly for material changes

requirements.requirement: Curate and maintain a library of role-specific technical training content covering current cybersecurity threats (e.g., OWASP, MITRE ATT&CK-aligned content), secure development practices, cloud security, incident response procedures, and risk management frameworks applicable to Malaysian financial services.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires training to enable staff to 'effectively perform their roles and responsibilities'. Generic training content fails to address the specific technical skills required for technology operations and cybersecurity in the Malaysian financial sector. Content must remain current as threats evolve.
requirements.frequency: Content reviewed and refreshed at minimum annually; threat-specific content updated as new threat intelligence emerges

requirements.requirement: Generate and retain structured training compliance reports, including completion rates by role category, certification status by individual, skills gap assessment results, and training expenditure versus plan. Reports must be archived for a minimum of 5 years to support BNM examination and internal audit requirements.
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory examinations will require documented evidence that training obligations have been met continuously. Without systematic reporting and retention, the institution cannot demonstrate compliance with the 'continuous' training obligation in the clause.
requirements.owner: Head of IT / Head of Technology Risk
requirements.frequency: Monthly reports generated; annual summary reported to Board Risk Committee; archived continuously

governance.summary: Governance structures must ensure that training and certification obligations under Clause 15.2 are policy-mandated, Board-visible, and subject to independent assurance. A formal Training and Competency Policy must codify requirements, ownership, and escalation paths, while the Board Risk Committee must receive periodic reporting on compliance posture. Internal audit must independently verify that training programmes are being executed as designed and that certification records are accurate.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a Board-approved Technology Staff Training and Competency Policy that defines: the scope of covered staff, minimum training hours and certification requirements by role, responsibilities of line managers and HR, escalation procedures for non-compliance, and the review cycle for the policy itself.
requirements.rationale: The clause imposes an obligation on 'a financial institution' as an entity, requiring institutional-level policy to operationalise the requirement. Without a formal policy, individual business units may interpret or apply the obligation inconsistently, creating uneven compliance across the institution.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer (CRO) as policy owner; Board Risk Committee for approval
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed and reaffirmed annually; updated when regulatory guidance or organisational structure changes

requirements.requirement: Establish a quarterly training compliance dashboard reported to the Board Risk Committee and senior management, covering: percentage of in-scope staff meeting minimum training requirements, certification attainment and lapse rates, skills gap assessment outcomes, and remediation actions for non-compliant individuals or teams.
requirements.rationale: Board oversight of training adequacy is essential to demonstrate that the institution at the highest governance level is monitoring compliance with this clause. The 'continuous' nature of the obligation requires periodic, not merely annual, escalation to the Board.
requirements.frequency: Quarterly reporting to Board Risk Committee; annual summary to full Board

requirements.requirement: Include technology staff training and certification compliance as a standing scope item in the Internal Audit annual plan, with findings reported to the Audit Committee. The audit must independently verify training records, certification validity, skills gap assessment outputs, and whether training content remains role-relevant.
requirements.rationale: Independent assurance is required to confirm that management reporting on training compliance is accurate and that the training programme delivers the competency outcomes the clause mandates. Internal audit provides the second line of defence validation necessary for BNM supervisory confidence.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor (CIA) / Head of Internal Audit
requirements.frequency: Full audit annually; targeted follow-up reviews if material gaps are identified

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal Training Governance Committee (or assign this mandate to an existing Technology Risk Committee) chaired by the CRO or CISO, responsible for approving annual training plans, reviewing certification requirements against evolving threat and regulatory landscapes, and adjudicating requests for training waivers or deferrals.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires training to be 'adequate and continuous', which demands an ongoing governance body with authority to adapt the training programme as the technology risk environment changes. A dedicated committee ensures training decisions are made at an appropriate seniority level and documented for audit purposes.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer (CRO) or Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) as Chair
requirements.frequency: Committee meets quarterly; ad hoc when significant regulatory or threat landscape changes occur

requirements.requirement: Incorporate third-party and outsourced technology service provider staff into the training and competency governance framework where those individuals perform functions equivalent to in-scope technology operations, cybersecurity, or risk management roles, including requiring contractual attestation of their certification status.
requirements.rationale: The clause applies to staff 'involved in technology operations, cybersecurity and risk management', which under RMiT's broader outsourcing provisions may extend to material third-party personnel with access to the institution's systems and data. Excluding them creates a governance gap that BNM examiners are likely to scrutinise.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer (CRO) in coordination with Head of Vendor/Third-Party Risk Management
requirements.frequency: Contractual attestation at onboarding and renewed annually; spot-checked during third-party audits

business.summary: Board members who lack current knowledge of technology developments are unable to provide credible oversight of technology strategy, risk appetite, and major IT investments, exposing the institution to governance failures and regulatory censure. This clause mandates a structured, recurring programme that equips directors with sufficient technological literacy to challenge management, approve technology-related risk decisions, and fulfil their fiduciary duties. Failure to comply risks regulatory sanctions, reputational damage, and the systemic risk of uninformed approval of flawed technology initiatives.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Technology Training Programme that delivers regular, structured training sessions covering material technology developments relevant to the institution, including emerging risks, digital transformation trends, and regulatory technology obligations.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text requires the institution to 'provide its board members with regular training and information on technology developments.' Without a formalised programme with defined content and scheduling, regularity cannot be demonstrated to BNM and board oversight effectiveness cannot be assured.
requirements.frequency: Minimum twice annually; supplemented by ad-hoc briefings on material technology events

requirements.requirement: Define and document a Board Technology Competency Framework that articulates the minimum level of technology literacy expected of board members, with a curriculum mapped to identified competency gaps and the institution's technology strategy.
requirements.rationale: Enabling the board 'to effectively discharge its oversight role' requires deliberate calibration of training content to actual competency needs. A framework ensures training is purposeful rather than ceremonial and provides an audit trail of intent and outcomes.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; updated whenever material changes occur to the institution's technology strategy or risk profile

requirements.requirement: Allocate a dedicated budget line item for board technology education, covering external subject matter experts, subscriptions to industry intelligence services, and attendance at relevant industry forums such as AIBIM or BNM-hosted events.
requirements.rationale: Delivering 'regular training and information' requires sustained financial commitment. Absence of a ring-fenced budget exposes the programme to deprioritisation during cost-cutting cycles, undermining the institution's ability to meet its regulatory obligation.
requirements.frequency: Annual budget cycle; reviewed mid-year

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual Board Technology Training Effectiveness Review to assess whether training has demonstrably improved the board's capacity to oversee technology strategy, risk, and major investment decisions, using pre/post assessments, board survey feedback, and quality of board challenge in Board IT/Risk Committee minutes.
requirements.rationale: The clause purpose is to 'enable the board to effectively discharge its oversight role,' meaning training delivery alone is insufficient — effectiveness must be measured. This review closes the loop between training input and governance output.
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Ensure that newly appointed board members receive a technology-focused onboarding briefing within 30 days of appointment, covering the institution's technology architecture, current risk landscape, digital strategy, and key regulatory technology obligations under RMiT.
requirements.rationale: The obligation to provide training applies to 'board members' collectively and continuously. New directors cannot fulfil their oversight role from day one without a baseline orientation, creating a gap period of governance risk if onboarding is delayed.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each new board appointment)

technology.summary: Technology teams must operationalise the board training programme by curating accurate, timely, and board-appropriate content on technology developments, and by maintaining systems that track training delivery and participation. The CTO and CISO are responsible for translating complex technical subjects into strategic narratives accessible to non-technical directors, ensuring the board receives intelligence on threats, innovations, and systemic risks that may affect the institution's technology posture. Supporting infrastructure must also ensure training records are audit-ready for BNM examination.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a Board Technology Briefing Pack — updated at least semi-annually — covering: current cyber threat landscape, major technology incidents in the Malaysian financial sector, digital innovation trends (e.g., AI/ML, cloud, open banking), and the institution's technology risk heatmap presented in business-impact terms.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text specifies 'training and information on technology developments.' For this information to be meaningful to the board, it must be curated, contextualised, and translated from technical detail into strategic insight by those with deep technical knowledge.
requirements.frequency: Semi-annual content refresh; ad-hoc updates for material events (e.g., major sector-wide cyber incidents)

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised Board Training Management System (or dedicated module within the institution's GRC platform) that records each training session, attendees, content covered, and completion status, capable of generating audit-ready reports for BNM examination.
requirements.rationale: BNM may request evidence that 'regular training' has been provided. Without a verifiable records system, the institution cannot demonstrate compliance, and the regulator has no basis to assess whether the board's oversight capability has been maintained.
requirements.frequency: Continuous maintenance; audit report generation on-demand and at each examination cycle

requirements.requirement: Establish a horizon-scanning process within the technology function to monitor emerging technology risks and regulatory technology developments (including BNM circulars, FSB publications, and SWIFT advisories) and feed material findings into board briefing materials within 30 days of identification.
requirements.rationale: The phrase 'technology developments' implies currency and relevance. Without a structured process for identifying what constitutes a significant development requiring board attention, the institution risks presenting outdated or incomplete information, impairing the board's oversight.
requirements.frequency: Continuous monitoring; monthly internal triage; material findings escalated within 30 days

requirements.requirement: Provide secure, board-appropriate access to a technology intelligence dashboard or curated briefing portal, enabling board members to access the latest institutional technology risk indicators, major incident summaries, and digital strategy progress between formal training sessions.
requirements.rationale: The requirement for 'regular information' suggests that periodic training sessions alone are insufficient — the board should have continuous access to relevant technology information to support ongoing informed oversight between formal sessions.
requirements.frequency: Platform maintained continuously; content refreshed monthly

requirements.requirement: Engage qualified external technology subject matter experts (e.g., cybersecurity specialists, cloud architects, or fintech strategists) to deliver specialist briefings on complex or rapidly evolving technology domains that internal teams may not present with sufficient objectivity or depth.
requirements.rationale: Internal teams may have blind spots or be subject to confirmation bias regarding the institution's own technology choices. External perspectives ensure board members receive balanced and credible 'information on technology developments,' which directly supports effective oversight.
requirements.frequency: Minimum annually; aligned to the training programme calendar

governance.summary: Governance structures must embed the board training obligation into formal policies, Board and Committee Terms of Reference, and the institution's accountability framework so that compliance is systematic rather than discretionary. The Board Chairman and Company Secretary bear primary accountability for ensuring training is scheduled, attended, and documented, with oversight cascaded through the Board Risk Committee and Technology Committee where applicable. Evidence of compliance must be maintained in a form that withstands BNM supervisory scrutiny.

requirements.requirement: Incorporate the obligation to provide regular board technology training into the institution's Board Technology Governance Policy or equivalent board-level policy document, specifying minimum training frequency, content categories, attendance expectations, and escalation procedures for non-attendance.
requirements.rationale: The word 'must' in 'a financial institution must provide' establishes a non-negotiable obligation. Embedding this in board-approved policy ensures the obligation is institutionalised, owned at the highest level, and not dependent on any individual's initiative to fulfil.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed annually; updated upon material regulatory changes

requirements.requirement: Mandate that the Board Risk Committee or Board Technology Committee include 'Board Technology Training Progress' as a standing agenda item at each meeting, reviewing the training schedule, attendance records, and any identified competency gaps.
requirements.rationale: Without formal committee oversight, board training can become tokenistic or lapse between reporting cycles. Embedding it as a standing agenda item ensures continuous governance attention, directly supporting the board's ability to 'effectively discharge its oversight role.'
requirements.frequency: Each committee meeting (minimum quarterly)

requirements.requirement: Require the Company Secretary to maintain a Board Technology Training Register, recording for each session: date, facilitator, topics covered, attendees, materials presented, and individual director attendance history, with retention for a minimum of seven years or as prescribed by BNM.
requirements.rationale: Documentary evidence is essential to demonstrate regulatory compliance. The Company Secretary, as the board's administrative officer, is the appropriate custodian of formal board records and provides the governance link between training activity and verifiable institutional records.
requirements.frequency: Updated after each training session; annual reconciliation

requirements.requirement: Include a summary of board technology training activities — covering sessions conducted, topics addressed, and aggregate attendance — in the institution's Annual Report or equivalent governance disclosure, and within internal board effectiveness assessments.
requirements.rationale: Transparency in reporting reinforces accountability and demonstrates to BNM and stakeholders that the institution takes its obligation to maintain an informed board seriously. It also creates an external accountability mechanism that reinforces internal compliance discipline.
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal escalation protocol whereby persistent non-attendance by individual board members at mandated technology training sessions is reported to the Nomination and Remuneration Committee for consideration in the board evaluation process, including potential reflection in director performance assessments.
requirements.rationale: The obligation is to provide training to 'board members' — implying active engagement, not mere availability. Without an accountability mechanism for non-attendance, the institution cannot demonstrate that individual directors are equipped to 'effectively discharge' their oversight roles, and BNM may question the board's collective competence.
requirements.owner: Board Nomination and Remuneration Committee Chairman
requirements.frequency: Triggered per-event; reviewed during annual board evaluation

16.1.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.1.title: Notification Prior to Digital Services Changes

business.summary: Financial institutions must establish a structured pre-launch notification process with BNM before any new digital service is introduced or any existing digital service is materially enhanced. Failure to notify exposes the institution to regulatory sanctions, potential suspension of the digital service, and reputational damage. Business units must embed this obligation into their digital product development lifecycle from ideation through to launch.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive Digital Services Register that catalogues all live digital services with version history, so that any planned new service or enhancement can be compared against the baseline to trigger the notification obligation under paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text distinguishes between 'new digital services' and 'enhancement to existing digital services'. Without a definitive register of current services, the institution cannot reliably determine which changes require notification, creating a compliance blind spot.
requirements.frequency: Continuous — updated at each change event; formally reviewed Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Define and document an institution-wide materiality threshold policy that distinguishes a notifiable 'enhancement to existing digital services' from a routine maintenance update or minor bug fix, ensuring consistent classification across all business lines.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates notification for enhancements but does not define the de minimis boundary. Without a clear internal threshold, business units may inconsistently self-exempt changes from notification, accumulating regulatory exposure over time.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed Annually; applied Per-event at each change classification decision

requirements.requirement: Integrate a mandatory BNM notification gate into the Digital Product Lifecycle process such that no new digital service or notifiable enhancement may progress to user acceptance testing or production deployment without documented evidence that the notification obligation under paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7 has been fulfilled.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim clause requires notification 'prior to' the introduction or enhancement, making this a pre-condition rather than a post-event report. A lifecycle gate enforces this sequencing and prevents inadvertent launch ahead of regulatory clearance.
requirements.frequency: Per-event — applied at each project stage gate

requirements.requirement: Assign a designated Regulatory Notification Coordinator within each business line responsible for preparing the notification package in accordance with paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7, liaising with the Compliance function, and tracking BNM's acknowledgement or response.
requirements.rationale: The clause imposes a positive obligation on the financial institution as a whole. Diffuse ownership across product teams without a named coordinator risks incomplete or late submissions, particularly for complex multi-channel digital services.
requirements.frequency: Role assignment reviewed Annually; duties exercised Per-event

requirements.requirement: Establish a forward-looking Digital Services Change Calendar, updated at least Quarterly, that aggregates planned new services and enhancements across all business units, enabling Compliance to plan notification submissions with adequate lead time before the required notification window.
requirements.rationale: The requirement to notify 'prior to' change implies a lead-time obligation defined further in paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7. Reactive, last-minute notification preparation increases the risk of submission errors and timeline breaches, especially for institutions with multiple concurrent digital initiatives.
requirements.frequency: Quarterly planning cycle; updated continuously as initiatives are scoped

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement automated change detection, classification, and workflow tooling to ensure that every candidate digital service change is captured, assessed for notifiability, and routed through the BNM notification process before deployment. Systems must also produce the audit-grade artefacts required to substantiate notifications submitted under paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7. This transforms the regulatory obligation from a manual compliance task into a governed, repeatable engineering process.

requirements.requirement: Implement a Change Classification Engine within the IT Service Management (ITSM) platform that automatically tags any change request touching customer-facing digital channels — internet banking, mobile banking, APIs exposed to third parties, and digital onboarding flows — as a candidate for regulatory notification review, triggering a structured compliance assessment workflow.
requirements.rationale: The clause covers both 'new digital services' and 'enhancements', requiring systematic identification at the point of change initiation rather than at deployment. Manual classification by developers is error-prone; automated tagging in the ITSM layer ensures no notifiable change is overlooked.
requirements.frequency: Continuous — evaluated at each change request submission

requirements.requirement: Implement deployment pipeline guardrails (CI/CD policy controls) that block promotion of any digital service change to production environments if the associated ITSM change record does not carry a verified 'BNM Notification Fulfilled' status flag, with override requiring dual authorisation from CTO and Chief Compliance Officer.
requirements.rationale: The clause's 'prior to' requirement mandates that notification precedes deployment. A pipeline-level enforcement control operationalises this sequencing at the point of technical execution, closing the gap between process policy and actual system behaviour.
requirements.frequency: Enforced Per-event at each production deployment; control effectiveness reviewed Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Maintain a versioned Technical Artefact Repository for all digital services, storing specifications, architecture diagrams, data flow maps, and risk assessments at each release, to serve as the evidentiary basis for notification packages submitted to BNM under paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7.
requirements.rationale: Notifications to BNM will require detailed technical documentation. Without a structured repository, assembling accurate, consistent documentation at notification time is delayed and increases the risk of submitting incomplete or inconsistent information to the regulator.
requirements.frequency: Updated Per-event at each release; repository integrity reviewed Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a Digital Services Inventory API or dashboard that provides real-time visibility into all production digital services, their current version, last modification date, and regulatory notification status, accessible to Compliance, Technology, and senior management.
requirements.rationale: Accurate identification of what constitutes an 'enhancement to existing digital services' requires a definitive, up-to-date view of the current service landscape. Stale or siloed inventories create classification disputes and notification gaps.
requirements.frequency: Maintained continuously; dashboard accuracy audited Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Integrate the notification workflow system with BNM's Regulatory Submission Portal (or equivalent official channel), maintaining a tamper-evident submission log that records notification date, content version, submission method, and any BNM correspondence, to provide an auditable evidence trail for examination purposes.
requirements.rationale: The obligation is to notify 'in accordance with the requirements in paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7', implying a formal, traceable submission. An integrated, logged submission mechanism provides the institution with defensible proof of compliance during regulatory examinations or in the event of a dispute about notification timeliness.
requirements.frequency: Per-event for each submission; log integrity reviewed Semi-annually

governance.summary: Governance structures must ensure that the BNM pre-notification obligation is embedded in board-level risk appetite, management committee decision-making, and internal audit scope. Senior management must own and be accountable for the notification programme, while independent assurance validates that no notifiable change is introduced without prior regulatory notification. Policy, accountability, and oversight must be codified and reviewed at least annually.

requirements.requirement: The Board Risk Committee must formally endorse and annually re-approve a Digital Services Notification Policy that defines the institution's obligations under clause 16.1, references the materiality threshold established under 16.1-B2, assigns accountability to named executives, and sets escalation triggers when notification timelines are at risk.
requirements.rationale: The clause imposes an obligation on the 'financial institution' as a legal entity, making board-level ownership essential. Board endorsement elevates the notification process from an operational procedure to a governed risk control, ensuring resources and authority are allocated commensurate with its regulatory significance.
requirements.frequency: Annually reviewed and re-approved; extraordinary review upon material change to BNM requirements

requirements.requirement: The Management Risk Committee or Technology Risk Committee must include a standing agenda item — at least Quarterly — to review the Digital Services Change Calendar, assess upcoming notification obligations, confirm that in-flight notifications are on track, and escalate any risk of late or missed notifications to the Board Risk Committee.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.1 creates a continuous, event-driven obligation. A quarterly committee review provides systematic senior management oversight that ensures emerging gaps between planned launches and notification readiness are identified and remediated before deployment deadlines are breached.
requirements.frequency: Quarterly at minimum; ad hoc when a new digital service initiative is approved

requirements.requirement: The Chief Compliance Officer must maintain a live Notification Obligation Tracker that records every notifiable digital service change, the target notification date, actual submission date, BNM reference number upon receipt, and final disposition, with a monthly status report distributed to the CEO, CTO, and Chief Digital Officer.
requirements.rationale: Effective governance of the 'prior to' requirement in clause 16.1 demands centralised visibility. Without a compliance-owned tracker, there is no single source of truth to confirm whether the institution has met its notification obligations, creating an audit and regulatory examination risk.
requirements.frequency: Maintained continuously; monthly reporting; annual reconciliation against the Digital Services Register

requirements.requirement: Internal Audit must conduct an annual thematic review of the institution's compliance with clause 16.1, examining whether all digital service introductions and enhancements during the review period were notified to BNM in advance, whether notifications met the requirements of paragraphs 16.2 to 16.7, and whether the lifecycle gate controls functioned as designed.
requirements.rationale: The 'S' (Standard) designation makes clause 16.1 a mandatory obligation subject to BNM examination. An independent annual audit provides the Board with assurance that the notification programme is operating effectively and identifies control failures before they are discovered by the regulator.
requirements.frequency: Annually; with interim spot-check sampling Semi-annually

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal Regulatory Breach Escalation Protocol specifically addressing late, missed, or erroneous BNM notifications, defining immediate remediation steps (including voluntary disclosure to BNM), root cause analysis requirements, corrective action ownership, and Board notification timelines, to minimise regulatory sanction risk when notification failures occur.
requirements.rationale: Even with robust controls, notification failures can occur. The clause's unconditional 'must notify' language leaves no safe harbour for inadvertent non-compliance. A pre-defined escalation protocol ensures the institution responds in a manner that demonstrates good faith to BNM and limits reputational and financial penalties.
requirements.frequency: Protocol reviewed Annually; invoked Per-event upon detection of a notification failure

16.2.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.2.title: First-Time Digital Services Notification

business.summary: Financial institutions launching digital services for the first time face a mandatory pre-launch notification obligation to BNM, covering risk posture, customer protections, and third-party arrangements. Failure to comply exposes the institution to regulatory sanctions, reputational damage, and the risk of operating unlicensed digital channels. The breadth of the notification package — spanning risk assessment through privacy policy — signals that BNM views first-time digital service launches as high-risk transformation events requiring structured management.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a comprehensive risk assessment prior to launching any digital service for the first time, identifying all material risks (operational, cyber, legal, reputational, third-party) and documenting specific risk management strategies, accountabilities, policies, and controls for each identified risk. This assessment must be packaged as a formal submission deliverable to BNM.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(a) explicitly requires 'assessment on the risks identified and strategies to manage such risks' including 'specific accountabilities, policies and controls.' Launching without this assessment means the institution cannot demonstrate to BNM that risks are understood and owned, creating potential for both regulatory breach and unmitigated operational losses.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each first-time digital service launch); annual refresh post-launch

requirements.requirement: Draft, review, and finalise all significant terms and conditions applicable to the digital service before submission to BNM. Terms must accurately reflect the service scope, fees, liability limitations, and customer obligations in plain language aligned with BNM consumer protection expectations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(c) mandates submission of 'significant terms and conditions' as part of the notification. Absent or deficient terms expose the institution to disputes, misselling risk, and regulatory scrutiny, particularly where digital services involve consumer lending, payments, or investment products.
requirements.owner: Chief Legal Officer (CLO) / Head of Legal and Compliance
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before each new digital service launch); reviewed whenever material service changes occur

requirements.requirement: Develop a client charter that articulates service standards, customer rights, complaint resolution timelines, and commitments on service availability and quality for the digital service being launched. The charter must be approved by senior management before being included in the BNM notification.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(d) specifically requires a 'client charter' as a notification element. BNM uses client charters to hold institutions accountable to stated customer service standards; an absent or vague charter undermines customer trust and creates regulatory exposure under BNM's consumer protection framework.
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Head of Customer Experience
requirements.frequency: Per-event (at launch); reviewed annually and upon material service scope changes

requirements.requirement: Draft and finalise a privacy policy statement that is specific to the digital service, disclosing the categories of personal data collected, purposes of processing, data sharing with third parties, retention periods, and customer rights under PDPA 2010. The policy must be completed and submitted to BNM prior to service go-live.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(e) requires a 'privacy policy statement' as part of the notification. Operating a digital service that collects customer data without a published, compliant privacy policy violates both BNM RMiT and Malaysia's PDPA 2010, compounding regulatory risk across two regulatory regimes.
requirements.owner: Data Protection Officer (DPO) / Chief Legal Officer (CLO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before launch); reviewed annually or upon changes to data processing activities

requirements.requirement: Compile and disclose to BNM all finalised outsourcing arrangements, website link arrangements, strategic alliances, and third-party partnerships that form part of or support the digital service delivery model. Disclosure must include the nature of each arrangement and the third party's role in the service chain.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(f) requires disclosure of 'any outsourcing or website link arrangements, or strategic alliances or partnerships with third parties that have been finalised.' Undisclosed third-party dependencies are a systemic risk vector; BNM's requirement ensures it can assess concentration risk and the institution's vendor oversight capability from the outset.
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Head of Vendor Management
requirements.frequency: Per-event (at launch); updated whenever material third-party arrangements change

technology.summary: Technology teams must design, implement, and document security controls and technical safeguards for the digital service before go-live, ensuring that the security arrangements submitted to BNM accurately reflect the deployed architecture. Additionally, technical due diligence on all third-party integrations — APIs, outsourced platforms, linked websites — must be completed and evidenced prior to submission. Ongoing technical monitoring and logging capabilities must be in place from day one to demonstrate control effectiveness post-notification.

requirements.requirement: Design and implement a comprehensive security control framework for the digital service covering authentication (MFA), encryption in transit and at rest, access controls, vulnerability management, intrusion detection, and DDoS mitigation. Produce a security architecture document that can be submitted to BNM as part of the 'security arrangements and controls' notification requirement.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(b) mandates submission of 'security arrangements and controls.' BNM expects security to be designed into the service architecture, not bolted on post-launch. Absence of documented controls is both a regulatory gap and a direct exposure to cyber incidents that could harm customers from the very first day of operation.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before launch); reviewed quarterly and after any material system changes or security incidents

requirements.requirement: Conduct technical risk assessments of the digital service infrastructure — including penetration testing, threat modelling, and vulnerability scanning — prior to launch. Results must feed directly into the risk assessment required under clause 16.2(a), with technical risks mapped to specific controls and owners.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(a) requires identification of risks and strategies to manage them with specific controls. Technical risks (e.g., application vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, API exposures) are material to digital services and must be identified through structured technical assessment, not assumed to be covered by policy alone.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before each new digital service launch); annual penetration testing thereafter; ad hoc after major releases

requirements.requirement: Perform technical due diligence on all third-party integrations, outsourced platforms, and linked websites that form part of the digital service. This includes API security reviews, assessment of third-party security certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2), SLA definitions, and data flow mapping for any customer data transiting third-party systems.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(f) requires disclosure of finalised outsourcing, website link, and third-party partnership arrangements. BNM expects institutions to understand the technical risk profile of their supply chain; undocumented data flows or insecure third-party APIs are systemic risks that regulators will scrutinise.
requirements.owner: Head of Technology / Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before each new third-party arrangement goes live); annually thereafter and upon contract renewal

requirements.requirement: Implement audit logging, monitoring dashboards, and alerting for the digital service from day one of go-live, covering authentication events, transaction anomalies, security events, and system availability. Logs must be retained in accordance with BNM's record-keeping requirements and must be demonstrably available for regulatory examination.
requirements.rationale: Supporting the risk controls documented under clause 16.2(a) and security arrangements under 16.2(b), continuous monitoring is necessary to demonstrate that controls are operational and effective post-notification. BNM can conduct supervisory reviews at any time and will expect evidence of active monitoring, not just documented intent.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (from service launch); monitoring rules reviewed quarterly

requirements.requirement: Publish the finalised privacy policy statement and client charter on the digital service's customer-facing interface (e.g., website, mobile app) before go-live, and implement technical mechanisms (e.g., consent checkboxes, version-controlled policy pages) to capture and record customer acknowledgement at onboarding.
requirements.rationale: Clauses 16.2(d) and 16.2(e) require a client charter and privacy policy statement. Publishing these documents is not sufficient without a technical mechanism to ensure customers have access to and acknowledge them; this also creates an auditable record for PDPA 2010 compliance and potential dispute resolution.
requirements.owner: Head of Digital / Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event (at launch); technical implementation reviewed annually or when policy content changes

governance.summary: Governance structures must ensure that the BNM notification is not treated as a compliance checkbox but as a board-level accountability event, with senior management formally approving each element of the notification package before submission. Clear ownership must be assigned to each notification component — risk assessment, security arrangements, T&Cs, client charter, privacy policy, and third-party disclosures — with evidence of review and sign-off maintained for regulatory examination. An ongoing governance mechanism must track the accuracy of notified arrangements and trigger re-notification obligations if material changes occur post-launch.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal governance process requiring Board or Board Risk Committee approval of the BNM notification package prior to submission. Board approval must be documented in board minutes, including explicit sign-off on the risk assessment, security arrangements, T&Cs, client charter, privacy policy, and third-party disclosure elements of clause 16.2.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(a) requires 'specific accountabilities' for managing identified risks; this must ultimately trace to the Board as the apex accountability body. BNM expects Board-level ownership of significant technology change events such as first-time digital service launches — absent Board engagement undermines the entire governance posture.
requirements.owner: Board Risk Committee Chairman / Board of Directors
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before each notification submission); Board briefing on post-launch performance quarterly

requirements.requirement: Assign named senior management owners (at minimum, C-suite or Head-of-function level) for each of the six notification elements in clause 16.2(a)–(f). Document accountability assignments in a RACI matrix approved by the CEO, and maintain an evidence trail showing each owner has reviewed and attested to the accuracy of their respective notification component.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(a) explicitly requires 'specific accountabilities' as part of the risk management documentation. Diffuse or undocumented ownership of notification components creates gaps where regulatory breaches can occur without clear consequence — BNM expects accountability to be named and traceable, not collective and anonymous.
requirements.owner: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event (assigned before each notification); reviewed annually and upon role changes

requirements.requirement: Develop or update internal policies governing digital service launches, requiring that all six elements of a clause 16.2 notification are completed and internally approved before any digital service is offered to customers for the first time. Policy must define escalation paths, timelines, and the role of Compliance in reviewing notification readiness.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(a) requires 'policies and controls to address risks.' Without an internal policy mandating pre-launch notification compliance, individual business units may launch digital services without triggering the BNM notification process, creating an undetected regulatory breach.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) / Head of Regulatory Compliance
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed annually; applied per-event at each new digital service launch

requirements.requirement: Establish a Third-Party Governance Committee or equivalent oversight function to review, approve, and maintain an inventory of all outsourcing arrangements, website links, strategic alliances, and partnerships disclosed under clause 16.2(f). This committee must assess the risk profile of each arrangement before it is finalised and included in the BNM notification.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.2(f) restricts disclosure to third-party arrangements 'that have been finalised,' implying a formal approval process must precede finalisation. Without structured third-party governance, arrangements may be executed ad hoc and either omitted from the BNM notification or included without adequate risk assessment, violating both clause 16.2(a) and 16.2(f).
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event (before each new arrangement is finalised); quarterly review of existing inventory

requirements.requirement: Implement a post-notification monitoring mechanism that tracks material changes to any element originally disclosed in the clause 16.2 notification — including changes to risk profile, security arrangements, T&Cs, client charter, privacy policy, or third-party arrangements — and triggers a structured review process to determine whether BNM re-notification or supplementary disclosure is required.
requirements.rationale: The clause 16.2 notification reflects a point-in-time snapshot of the institution's digital service posture. Without a governance mechanism to monitor post-launch changes, the information held by BNM becomes stale, undermining the regulator's supervisory oversight and potentially leaving the institution in constructive non-compliance as its actual arrangements diverge from what was notified.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Continuous monitoring; formal review quarterly and upon any material change to notified arrangements

16.3.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.3.title: Simplified Notification for Enhancements

business.summary: Financial institutions must maintain an operationally ready process to identify enhancements to existing digital services that qualify under Appendix 6 and trigger a simplified notification to BNM. Failure to submit timely and complete notifications—even under the simplified pathway—constitutes a regulatory breach that can attract supervisory action, reputational damage, and potential licence conditions. Business units sponsoring digital enhancements bear accountability for initiating the notification workflow before deployment.

requirements.requirement: Establish and operationalise a pre-deployment screening process for all digital service enhancements to determine whether they meet the Appendix 6 criteria that trigger the simplified notification obligation under Clause 16.3.
requirements.rationale: The clause conditions the simplified pathway on enhancements meeting Appendix 6 criteria. Without a structured screening step, the institution risks either missing the notification obligation entirely or defaulting to the full notification process unnecessarily, causing delays to go-live.
requirements.owner: Chief Digital Officer / Head of Digital Banking
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each enhancement release cycle)

requirements.requirement: Produce and submit to BNM a clear, non-technical description of each qualifying enhancement, covering the scope of changes to existing technologies, the affected digital services, and the expected business impact, as required by Clause 16.3(a).
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.3(a) explicitly mandates a description of enhancements as part of the simplified notification content. An incomplete or vague description does not satisfy the regulatory requirement and may prompt BNM to request a full notification, delaying product launch.
requirements.owner: Head of Product Management / Chief Operating Officer
requirements.frequency: Per-event (for each qualifying enhancement notification)

requirements.requirement: Commission and submit a formal risk assessment for each qualifying enhancement, identifying material risks, their potential impact on customers and operations, and the specific measures adopted to mitigate those risks, as mandated by Clause 16.3(b).
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.3(b) is explicit: the notification must include a risk assessment covering both impact and mitigation measures. Absence of either element renders the notification incomplete and could be treated as a failure to notify, attracting enforcement attention.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (concurrent with enhancement screening and submission preparation)

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised notification register logging all simplified notifications submitted under Clause 16.3, including submission dates, enhancement descriptions, risk assessment summaries, and BNM acknowledgement records.
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory reviews and on-site examinations routinely require evidence of prior notifications. A complete register demonstrates a culture of compliance, supports regulatory dialogue, and provides an audit trail if the adequacy of past notifications is questioned.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (updated upon each submission and BNM response)

requirements.requirement: Define and enforce a mandatory lead time before go-live by which the simplified notification package must be submitted to BNM, ensuring BNM receives the notification within any prescribed window and that the institution does not deploy the enhancement prior to meeting the notification obligation.
requirements.rationale: The simplified pathway does not eliminate the obligation to notify prior to or concurrent with deployment. Without a defined submission lead time embedded in project governance, business pressure can result in late or post-facto notifications, constituting a regulatory breach.
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer / Head of Programme Management Office
requirements.frequency: Per-event (enforced at project gate reviews)

technology.summary: Technology teams must embed regulatory notification triggers directly into the change and release management lifecycle, ensuring that qualifying digital service enhancements automatically initiate the Clause 16.3 documentation workflow. Systems must support structured capture of enhancement descriptions and machine-readable risk assessments, with audit trails that can demonstrate compliance to BNM examiners. Integration between change management tooling and the compliance notification workflow is essential to prevent enhancements from being deployed without fulfilling the simplified notification requirement.

requirements.requirement: Integrate an Appendix 6 qualification check as a mandatory gate within the institution's change management and IT project intake system, automatically flagging enhancements to existing digital services that meet the defined criteria and routing them through the Clause 16.3 simplified notification workflow.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.3 applies specifically to enhancements meeting Appendix 6 criteria. Without automated classification at the point of change request, qualifying enhancements can inadvertently bypass the notification obligation, creating a systemic compliance gap across hundreds of change requests per year.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (operational at all times; triggered per change request)

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain standardised digital templates for the Clause 16.3(a) enhancement description, capturing the existing technology baseline, the nature and scope of changes, affected customer-facing digital services, and technical dependencies, to ensure submissions are consistent and complete.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.3(a) requires a description of enhancements to existing technologies. Standardised templates reduce the risk of omissions, accelerate preparation, and ensure that submissions across different product teams are comparable and acceptable to BNM reviewers.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Governance / Head of Enterprise Architecture
requirements.frequency: Per-event (template applied to each qualifying enhancement); template reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Implement a structured technology risk assessment tool or module—integrated with the change management system—that guides project teams through identification of risks arising from the proposed enhancement, quantification of impact, and documentation of specific technical and operational mitigation controls, satisfying Clause 16.3(b).
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.3(b) mandates a risk assessment covering impact and mitigation measures. An unstructured or ad hoc approach produces inconsistent outputs that may not meet BNM's expectations. A guided tool ensures completeness, repeatability, and auditability of risk assessments across all qualifying changes.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (completed for each qualifying enhancement prior to submission)

requirements.requirement: Maintain tamper-evident audit logs and version-controlled records of all Clause 16.3 notification packages—including enhancement descriptions, risk assessments, approval sign-offs, submission timestamps, and BNM correspondence—stored in a secure document management system with role-based access control.
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory examinations require demonstrable evidence that notifications were submitted as required. Immutable audit logs protect the institution from disputes about submission timing or content, and support internal second-line and third-line assurance reviews.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Operations / Head of Information Security
requirements.frequency: Continuous (records retained per BNM record-keeping requirements, minimum 7 years)

requirements.requirement: Implement a deployment gate control that blocks or escalates release of any qualifying digital service enhancement to production environments unless the Clause 16.3 simplified notification submission has been confirmed and recorded in the change management system.
requirements.rationale: Without a hard deployment gate, time-to-market pressure can result in enhancements going live before the notification obligation is met. Linking deployment approval to confirmed notification submission enforces compliance at the technical execution layer, not just at the policy level.
requirements.owner: Head of DevOps / Head of Release Management
requirements.frequency: Continuous (enforced at each production deployment event)

governance.summary: Governance structures must formalise the institution's approach to identifying, preparing, approving, and submitting simplified notifications under Clause 16.3, with clear ownership at senior management level and board visibility over the programme. Policies must define the Appendix 6 screening criteria, escalation pathways, and submission timelines, while committee oversight ensures that risk assessments submitted to BNM are independently reviewed and not solely the product of the business unit proposing the enhancement. Periodic assurance activities must verify that the simplified notification process is functioning as designed.

requirements.requirement: Formulate and approve a Regulatory Notification Policy that explicitly codifies the simplified notification process under Clause 16.3, including: criteria mapping from Appendix 6, ownership of screening decisions, required content for submissions under Clause 16.3(a) and 16.3(b), and submission timelines relative to enhancement deployment.
requirements.rationale: Without a documented policy, each business unit or project team may interpret the obligation differently, resulting in inconsistent compliance. A formalised policy creates a single, auditable standard and establishes clear accountability that BNM can assess during supervisory reviews.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer / Board Risk Committee
requirements.frequency: Policy approved annually and updated upon each BNM regulatory amendment

requirements.requirement: Establish a cross-functional Digital Notification Review Committee (or embed the function within an existing Technology Risk or Change Advisory Board) with defined authority to review, challenge, and approve simplified notification packages before submission to BNM, ensuring independent validation of both the enhancement description and the risk assessment.
requirements.rationale: Clause 16.3(b) requires a risk assessment of proposed enhancements. Governance requires that this assessment be subject to independent review—not solely prepared and approved by the sponsoring business unit—to ensure objectivity and adequacy of identified risks and mitigations.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer / Head of Compliance
requirements.frequency: Per-event (convened or consulted for each qualifying enhancement prior to submission)

requirements.requirement: Assign a named Regulatory Notification Coordinator role (within the Compliance or Regulatory Affairs function) responsible for maintaining the notification register, liaising with BNM on all Clause 16.3 submissions, tracking BNM acknowledgements, and escalating any BNM queries or concerns to senior management within defined SLA timeframes.
requirements.rationale: Without designated ownership of the end-to-end notification lifecycle, submissions can fall between organisational silos. A named coordinator ensures accountability, continuity, and a single point of contact for BNM, reducing the risk of missed submissions or unresponded BNM queries.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer / Head of Regulatory Affairs
requirements.frequency: Continuous (role is ongoing); performance reviewed quarterly

requirements.requirement: Include a standing agenda item in the Technology Risk Committee or equivalent governance forum for a quarterly review of all simplified notifications submitted under Clause 16.3 in the preceding period, covering completeness, timeliness, risk assessment quality, and any BNM feedback received.
requirements.rationale: Quarterly oversight provides senior management with visibility over the volume and quality of notifications, identifies systemic gaps in the screening or documentation process, and demonstrates to BNM that the institution applies active governance to its notification obligations—not merely reactive compliance.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer / Technology Risk Committee Chairperson
requirements.frequency: Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Commission an annual internal audit or independent compliance review of the Clause 16.3 simplified notification process, assessing whether all qualifying enhancements were correctly identified, notifications were complete and timely, risk assessments were adequate, and records are maintained in accordance with BNM requirements.
requirements.rationale: Third-line assurance is essential to verify that first-line (business) and second-line (risk and compliance) controls over the Clause 16.3 process are functioning effectively. Annual audit findings must be reported to the Board Audit Committee, supporting the institution's accountability to BNM for sustained compliance.
requirements.owner: Head of Internal Audit / Board Audit Committee
requirements.frequency: Annual

16.4.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.4.title: Assurance and Confirmation for Material Enhancements

business.summary: Clause 16.4 mandates that financial institutions seeking to roll out material digital service enhancements obtain both independent external assurance and senior leadership confirmation before notifying BNM. Failure to comply exposes the institution to regulatory sanctions, reputational damage, and the risk of launching inadequately secured digital services to customers. The sequencing requirement — assurance and confirmation must precede BNM notification — makes this a hard gate in any digital programme delivery lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal pre-notification checklist and programme gate that mandates completion of external assurance (Appendix 7 Part A) and senior leadership confirmation (Appendix 7 Part B) before any BNM notification is filed for material digital service enhancements.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text explicitly states these measures must be undertaken 'prior to notifying the Bank', making them mandatory programme gates rather than optional quality checks. Without a formal gate, project teams risk notifying BNM prematurely, triggering regulatory non-compliance.
requirements.owner: Chief Digital Officer / Chief Operating Officer
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each material digital service enhancement)

requirements.requirement: Define and document internal criteria distinguishing 'material enhancements' subject to clause 16.4 from enhancements qualifying for simplified notification under clause 16.3, and embed this classification step into the project intake process.
requirements.rationale: The clause applies only to enhancements 'not subject to the simplified notification requirement under paragraph 16.3'. A clear, documented classification methodology prevents misrouting of enhancements — either under-reporting material changes or over-burdening minor changes with full assurance requirements.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (project intake), with classification criteria reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Procure and maintain a panel of qualified independent external assurance providers capable of assessing technology risks and security controls for digital services, with contracts structured to accommodate on-demand engagement aligned to project delivery timelines.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires engagement of an 'independent external party', which necessitates pre-qualified vendor arrangements to avoid schedule delays. Last-minute procurement of external assurance providers frequently causes programme timeline overruns and may compromise assurance quality.
requirements.owner: Chief Procurement Officer / Chief Operating Officer
requirements.frequency: Panel reviewed annually; engaged per-event for each material enhancement

requirements.requirement: Allocate dedicated budget lines in digital programme business cases for independent external assurance costs, treating external assurance as a non-negotiable regulatory cost of delivery for all material digital service enhancements.
requirements.rationale: External assurance engagements carry material cost. Without explicit budget allocation at programme inception, project teams may attempt to substitute internal reviews for the required independent external party, creating regulatory exposure.
requirements.frequency: Per programme business case approval cycle

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised register of all digital service enhancements, recording their clause 16.3 or 16.4 classification, assurance engagement status, confirmation signatory details, and BNM notification dates to provide an auditable compliance trail.
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory reviews and internal audits will require evidence that all material enhancements underwent the prescribed process. A centralised register demonstrates systematic compliance and enables rapid response to regulatory enquiries.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (updated per-event); reviewed quarterly

technology.summary: Technology teams must ensure that digital service enhancement projects incorporate structured technology risk assessments and security control validations in a form suitable for independent external review using the Appendix 7 Part A format. This requires mature security testing, documentation, and evidence-gathering practices embedded within the software development and change management lifecycle. The CISO holds direct accountability for the technical evidence underpinning both the external assurance and the internal confirmation.

requirements.requirement: Embed a Technology Risk and Security Control Assessment (TRSCA) artefact into the definition-of-done for all digital service enhancement projects classified as material, producing documented evidence of identified risks, applied controls, and residual risk acceptance — structured to directly support Appendix 7 Part A external assurance.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires an independent external party to provide assurance that 'technology risks and security controls associated with the digital services' have been addressed. Without pre-prepared, structured risk and control evidence, external assurers cannot conduct an efficient or conclusive review.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each material enhancement); artefact template reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Conduct independent penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and security architecture reviews for every material digital service enhancement, with findings formally remediated or risk-accepted before external assurance engagement commences.
requirements.rationale: External assurance providers (Appendix 7 Part A) will assess whether security controls have been addressed. Outstanding unmitigated vulnerabilities or untested attack surfaces will prevent assurance from being granted, delaying BNM notification and launch. Remediation must precede — not run in parallel with — external assurance.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each material enhancement)

requirements.requirement: Implement a document management workflow that tracks the preparation, review, and sign-off of Appendix 7 Part A assurance reports and Part B confirmation letters, with version control, access logging, and tamper-evident storage to support regulatory audit.
requirements.rationale: The clause specifies that assurance and confirmation must follow prescribed formats ('as set out in Part A of Appendix 7' and 'Part B of Appendix 7'). A controlled document management process ensures format compliance, prevents unauthorised amendments, and provides an auditable record of the regulatory submission package.
requirements.frequency: Per-event; system capability reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal external assurance readiness review, conducted by internal security and technology risk teams, as a pre-gate prior to engaging the independent external party — verifying that all required technical evidence, test results, and control documentation are complete and aligned to Appendix 7 Part A requirements.
requirements.rationale: Incomplete or poorly structured evidence packages submitted to external assurers result in iterative information requests, extending timelines and increasing cost. An internal readiness gate ensures the external engagement is efficient and conclusive, reducing the risk of delayed BNM notification.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each material enhancement)

requirements.requirement: Maintain a real-time digital services inventory that records the security assurance status, last external review date, active enhancements in progress, and BNM notification history for each digital service, enabling the CISO to discharge their confirmation responsibilities under Appendix 7 Part B with current, accurate information.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the CISO to personally confirm the institution's readiness. This confirmation carries professional and regulatory accountability. A live, accurate inventory ensures the CISO's confirmation is evidence-based rather than reliant on informal assurances from project teams.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (real-time updates); reviewed monthly by CISO

governance.summary: Clause 16.4 establishes a dual-layer governance confirmation requirement: independent external assurance from outside the institution, and formal internal confirmation from the CISO, a senior management officer, or the board chairman or designated board-level committee. This reflects BNM's expectation that material digital service enhancements carry both technical and strategic accountability at the highest levels of the institution. Governance structures must formalise who signs, under what conditions, and with what supporting evidence, to ensure confirmations are substantive rather than procedural.

requirements.requirement: Define and document the authorised signatory matrix for Appendix 7 Part B confirmations, specifying the conditions under which the CISO, a senior management officer, or the board chairman or designated board-level committee (as referenced in paragraph 8.3) provides the confirmation, including delegation thresholds and escalation triggers.
