requirements.rationale: The clause offers three confirmation signatory options — CISO, senior management officer, or board chairman/committee — without specifying which applies when. An ambiguous signatory matrix risks either under-escalation (CISO signing off changes that warrant board visibility) or over-escalation (board involvement for routine enhancements), neither of which serves effective governance.
requirements.frequency: Matrix established at policy level; reviewed annually and upon organisational changes

requirements.requirement: Incorporate the Appendix 7 Part B confirmation as a standing agenda item in the Technology Risk Committee or designated board-level committee for all material digital service enhancements escalated to board level, with supporting evidence packages (external assurance report, risk assessment summary, residual risk position) provided to committee members at least five business days prior to the meeting.
requirements.rationale: The clause links the confirmation authority to the 'chairman of the board or designated board-level committee specified in paragraph 8.3', indicating BNM's expectation that board-level governance actively reviews material digital risks rather than rubber-stamping submissions. Adequate notice periods and evidence packages are necessary for informed confirmation.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each material enhancement requiring board-level confirmation)

requirements.requirement: Establish a policy mandating that no BNM notification for a material digital service enhancement is submitted until both the signed Appendix 7 Part A external assurance report and the signed Appendix 7 Part B senior confirmation are in hand, with the Compliance function acting as the notification control point.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text states these measures must be completed 'prior to notifying the Bank', making sequencing a regulatory requirement. A compliance-controlled submission gate prevents inadvertent early notification by project or technology teams who may be unaware of the regulatory sequencing obligation.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each material enhancement); policy reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual internal audit review of all clause 16.4 compliance activities for the preceding year, assessing completeness of external assurance engagements, appropriateness of signatory escalation, format adherence to Appendix 7, and timeliness of BNM notifications relative to assurance and confirmation completion dates.
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory examinations will test whether institutions have consistently applied clause 16.4 requirements across all material enhancements, not just in isolated instances. A structured annual internal audit provides early detection of systemic gaps and demonstrates the institution's commitment to ongoing regulatory compliance.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor / Audit Committee Chairman
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a clause 16.4 compliance procedure document, referencing the current Appendix 7 Part A and Part B formats, the paragraph 8.3 committee designation, and the clause 16.3 materiality threshold criteria, and ensure all relevant stakeholders (digital programme leads, CISO's office, legal, compliance) are trained on this procedure upon onboarding and whenever regulatory guidance is updated.
requirements.rationale: The clause references multiple interconnected RMiT provisions (paragraphs 16.3, 8.3, and Appendix 7). Staff unfamiliar with these cross-references may misclassify enhancements, use incorrect assurance formats, or engage the wrong confirmation signatory. A maintained procedure document and training programme mitigate these operational compliance risks.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer / Head of Regulatory Affairs
requirements.frequency: Procedure reviewed at least annually and upon BNM RMiT amendments; training delivered at onboarding and upon material updates

16.5.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.5.title: Independent External Party Assurance Quality

business.summary: Financial institutions must engage only qualified and proven external assurance providers when introducing or enhancing digital services, failing which BNM may reject the notification or impose remedial action. Poor assurance quality introduces undetected operational, cyber, and technology risks that could disrupt digital services, harm customers, and attract regulatory sanctions. The scope of assurance is non-negotiable: it must explicitly cover all matters in Appendix 7 Parts C and D, and any gaps in coverage render the assurance invalid for BNM submission purposes.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal pre-engagement qualification process to verify that any external assurance party is competent to assess digital financial services in the Malaysian regulatory context, including relevant certifications (e.g., CISA, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor, CBEST), industry experience, and demonstrated familiarity with BNM RMiT requirements before engagement.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly requires that the external party be 'competent', which is an affirmative duty on the institution — not merely a best-efforts obligation. Engaging an underqualified party exposes the institution to the risk of an inadequate assurance report that does not satisfy BNM, triggering rejection of the digital service notification.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each new assurance engagement)

requirements.requirement: Conduct a track record assessment of prospective external assurance providers, including reference checks from at least two prior financial institution clients in Malaysia or comparable jurisdictions, review of past assurance report samples, and any adverse findings from regulatory bodies or professional associations.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates a 'good track record', which requires the institution to exercise due diligence on historical performance. A provider with a weak or undocumented track record in assessing digital financial services represents a business risk — their report may be challenged by BNM as insufficiently credible.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each new assurance engagement); refreshed at least every two years for panel providers

requirements.requirement: Define the assurance scope in the engagement letter to explicitly map to and cover all matters specified in Appendix 7 Part C (technology risk assessment matters) and Part D (cybersecurity assessment matters), ensuring no material item is excluded or scoped out prior to BNM notification submission.
requirements.rationale: The clause states the assurance 'shall address the matters covered in, and comply with, Part C and D of Appendix 7'. A scope gap — even if commercially agreed between the institution and the provider — is a regulatory non-compliance. Business owners must understand that scope negotiation cannot dilute mandatory regulatory coverage.
requirements.owner: Chief Digital Officer (CDO) / Head of Digital Banking
requirements.frequency: Per-event (at commencement of each assurance engagement)

requirements.requirement: Maintain an approved panel of qualified external assurance providers and review panel membership annually, removing any provider whose competency, track record, or independence has deteriorated, and onboarding new providers through the full qualification process.
requirements.rationale: Relying on ad-hoc provider selection per engagement increases the risk of engaging an unqualified party under time pressure when a digital service launch is imminent. A pre-vetted panel ensures business agility without compromising on the clause's competency and track record requirements.
requirements.frequency: Annual panel review; per-event additions

requirements.requirement: Include contractual warranties in the engagement agreement obligating the external party to confirm their competency, disclose any conflicts of interest, and warrant that the assurance report will comply with Appendix 7 Parts C and D in its entirety, with the institution retaining the right to reject and seek remediation at no additional cost if the report is found non-compliant.
requirements.rationale: Competency and track record at the time of selection may not guarantee a compliant deliverable. Contractual protections align the external party's commercial incentives with the institution's regulatory obligations, and provide recourse if the assurance report is rejected by BNM due to non-compliance with Appendix 7.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (at contract execution)

technology.summary: Technology teams must ensure that the assurance engagement is technically scoped to cover all systems, infrastructure, and controls relevant to the digital service being introduced or enhanced, aligned to the specific technical matters required under Appendix 7 Parts C and D. IT and cybersecurity teams are responsible for providing the external assurance party with accurate, complete technical documentation and access necessary to conduct the assessment, and must track remediation of findings arising from the assurance report. A vendor registry with competency and track record data for approved external assurance providers must be maintained to support rapid and compliant provider selection.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a structured technical evidence package for each digital service notification, including architecture diagrams, data flow diagrams, access control matrices, vulnerability assessment reports, penetration test results, and control self-assessments, to be made available to the external assurance party in a controlled manner to enable a thorough assessment of Appendix 7 Part C and D matters.
requirements.rationale: The external party can only deliver a competent and compliant assurance report if the institution provides complete and accurate technical inputs. Gaps in technical documentation provided to the assessor will result in gaps in the assurance report's coverage of Appendix 7 requirements, creating a regulatory compliance failure that originates from inadequate IT support of the assurance process.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (assembled prior to each assurance engagement commencement)

requirements.requirement: Implement a secure, access-controlled data room or virtual collaboration environment for sharing technical documentation with the external assurance party, with full audit logging of all documents accessed, downloaded, or shared, and automatic expiry of access upon engagement completion.
requirements.rationale: Sharing sensitive technical documentation (architecture, security controls, vulnerability data) with an external party introduces confidentiality and data integrity risks. A controlled sharing environment ensures that the institution fulfils its obligation to enable a comprehensive assessment while maintaining information security commensurate with the sensitivity of materials related to digital financial services.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (provisioned and deprovisioned per engagement); reviewed annually for platform security

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain an internal checklist mapped to each specific matter in Appendix 7 Part C and Part D, enabling the technology team to verify that the external assurance party's report addresses every required element before the report is accepted and submitted to BNM as part of the notification.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the assurance to 'comply with' Appendix 7 Parts C and D — this is an outcome requirement, not merely a process requirement. A technology-side compliance checklist provides a systematic mechanism to detect coverage gaps in the report before regulatory submission, preventing BNM rejection or post-submission scrutiny.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Risk / Head of Cybersecurity
requirements.frequency: Per-event (applied upon receipt of draft assurance report); checklist reviewed annually or upon RMiT amendment

requirements.requirement: Establish a findings remediation tracking system to log, assign, track, and close all technology-related findings identified in the external assurance report, with defined SLAs for critical and high findings, escalation paths for overdue items, and integration with the institution's IT risk register.
requirements.rationale: An assurance report that identifies control gaps must translate into remediated controls, not just a filed document. The competency and track record requirements imply that the assurance will surface real issues; the institution's technical teams must have a disciplined mechanism to act on findings, particularly those relating to Appendix 7 Part C and D matters which directly relate to technology risk and cybersecurity.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer (CIO) / Head of IT Operations
requirements.frequency: Continuous (from report issuance through to finding closure); quarterly status reporting

requirements.requirement: Maintain a vendor registry with structured profiles for each approved external assurance provider, capturing certifications held, expiry dates, past engagements with Malaysian financial institutions, regulatory or professional body sanctions history, and technical specialisations relevant to Appendix 7 Part C and D assessment areas, updated at each annual panel review.
requirements.rationale: Competency and track record assessments cannot be repeated from scratch for every engagement under time pressure. A maintained technical registry enables rapid, evidence-based provider selection while satisfying the clause's requirement that competency and track record be positively established — with an auditable trail for BNM examination if challenged.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Risk / Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
requirements.frequency: Annual refresh; updated upon each new provider onboarding or adverse event

governance.summary: The Board and senior management must establish governance structures that ensure the selection, oversight, and accountability of external assurance providers are not left to individual business units but are governed by institution-wide policy, committee oversight, and formal approval processes. Governance must ensure that assurance scope is protected from commercial or operational pressures that might lead to scope reduction incompatible with Appendix 7 Parts C and D. The institution must also maintain audit trails demonstrating that the competency and track record requirements were actively applied — not merely asserted — to satisfy potential BNM supervisory examination.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a Board-approved policy on External Technology Assurance that defines minimum competency criteria for external assurance providers (including required qualifications, years of experience, and sector relevance), the track record assessment methodology, approved scope boundaries aligned to Appendix 7 Parts C and D, and the authority matrix for approving provider selection and assurance reports.
requirements.rationale: The clause places an affirmative obligation on the 'financial institution' as an entity — not on any individual — to ensure competency and track record of the external party. Without a governing policy, selection decisions are inconsistent, undocumented, and indefensible under BNM examination. Board approval of the policy anchors accountability at the highest governance level.
requirements.owner: Board Risk Committee (BRC) / Board of Directors
requirements.frequency: Policy approved upon initial adoption; reviewed annually and upon material RMiT updates

requirements.requirement: Mandate that the approval of an external assurance provider for any digital service notification engagement be subject to a formal approval process at a governance committee level (minimum: Risk Management Committee or Technology Risk Committee), with documented rationale evidencing the competency and track record assessment outcomes, prior to engagement commencement.
requirements.rationale: Delegating provider selection solely to the business or technology unit creating the digital service creates a conflict of interest — the same team under pressure to launch may select a less rigorous but faster or cheaper provider. Committee-level approval ensures independent governance oversight and generates an auditable approval record demonstrating active compliance with the clause's requirements.
requirements.owner: Risk Management Committee (RMC) / Technology Risk Committee
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each engagement commencement)

requirements.requirement: Implement a formal quality assurance review of the external assurance report by the institution's internal audit or technology risk function before the report is accepted and incorporated into the BNM notification, specifically verifying that the report addresses and complies with every matter stipulated in Appendix 7 Parts C and D and that the report reflects the scope defined in the engagement letter.
requirements.rationale: The clause's requirement that the assurance 'comply with' Appendix 7 Parts C and D is an institutional obligation — accepting a non-compliant report and submitting it to BNM makes the institution liable for the deficiency. An independent internal quality check before acceptance creates a second line of defence against assurance report deficiencies attributable to the external party's failure to deliver a compliant product.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor / Head of Technology Risk
requirements.frequency: Per-event (upon receipt of the final or near-final assurance report)

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centrally managed register of all external assurance engagements conducted under clause 16.5, recording the provider selected, the competency and track record evidence relied upon for selection, the assurance scope, the date and outcome of the internal quality review, findings summary, remediation status, and BNM notification reference, retained for a minimum of seven years.
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory examinations may occur years after a digital service has launched, and the institution must be able to demonstrate on demand that the external party was competent, had a good track record, and that the assurance addressed Appendix 7 Parts C and D. Without a central register, evidence may be fragmented across business units or lost through staff turnover, rendering the institution unable to substantiate compliance.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (updated per engagement); register integrity reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Report annually to the Board Risk Committee on the quality and performance of external assurance providers, including any instances where assurance reports were rejected or required material revision due to competency or scope deficiencies, panel refresh outcomes, and any emerging regulatory expectations regarding Appendix 7 assurance standards.
requirements.rationale: Board-level visibility of external assurance quality enables the Board to exercise its oversight obligation over technology risk governance and to direct management action where systemic weaknesses in provider quality are identified. This reporting loop also ensures that the institution's governance framework remains responsive to evolving BNM supervisory expectations without requiring clause-by-clause policy rewrites.
requirements.frequency: Annual (to Board Risk Committee); ad-hoc escalation for material failures

16.6.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.6.title: Provision of Documents for Bank Review

business.summary: Financial institutions must maintain readiness to produce relevant documentation supporting any notified digital service introduction or enhancement upon BNM's request, failing which the institution risks regulatory sanctions, supervisory escalation, or suspension of the digital service in question. The obligation is demand-triggered but preparation must be continuous, meaning operational teams cannot treat documentation as a post-launch afterthought. Non-compliance exposes the institution to reputational damage, enforcement action under DFIA 2013, and loss of BNM's trust in the institution's governance maturity.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a comprehensive document inventory for every notified digital service introduction or enhancement, capturing all artefacts that BNM may reasonably request, including business cases, risk assessments, vendor contracts, test evidence, and post-launch monitoring reports.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim obligation to 'provide relevant documents' presupposes that such documents exist and are retrievable on demand. Without a maintained inventory, the institution cannot respond promptly to BNM requests, creating compliance exposure under this Standard clause.
requirements.owner: Chief Digital Officer / Head of Digital Banking
requirements.frequency: Per-event (at each digital service notification), with quarterly inventory reconciliation

requirements.requirement: Define and operationalise a BNM document-request response procedure with a maximum internal response SLA (not exceeding 5 business days for standard requests), clear escalation paths, and a designated relationship-management focal point accountable for liaising with BNM during review exercises.
requirements.rationale: The phrase 'when required by the Bank' implies an externally imposed, potentially urgent timeline. Without a documented internal SLA and escalation chain, response coordination defaults to ad-hoc handling, increasing the risk of delayed or incomplete submissions.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each BNM request); SLA reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Integrate document-readiness checkpoints into the digital service launch lifecycle gate process, ensuring no digital service is introduced or enhanced without confirmed availability of all regulatory-submission artefacts in the institutional document repository.
requirements.rationale: Producing 'relevant documents' reactively after a BNM request is high-risk; documents assembled under time pressure are prone to gaps. Embedding readiness gates at pre-launch ensures documents are review-ready at the moment of notification.
requirements.owner: Head of Digital Banking / Chief Operating Officer
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each digital service change lifecycle)

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual simulation exercises (tabletop or dry-run) that replicate a BNM document-review request scenario, measuring the institution's ability to locate, compile, and transmit required documents within the defined internal SLA.
requirements.rationale: Regulatory preparedness must be tested, not assumed. Simulation exercises surface gaps in the document inventory, ownership accountability, and transmission procedures before an actual BNM request exposes them.
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Maintain a formal document-submission log recording all instances where documents were provided to BNM under this clause, including the request date, documents submitted, submission date, and the name of the authorising officer, to support audit trail and regulatory relationship management.
requirements.rationale: The 'Bank's review' obligation creates a recurring compliance event that must be evidenced. A submission log demonstrates institutional responsiveness and supports internal audit, CRO reporting, and future BNM inspections.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (updated per submission); reviewed quarterly

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement a secure, auditable, and rapidly searchable document management infrastructure capable of storing, versioning, and retrieving all artefacts related to digital service notifications on demand. The system must support controlled external transmission to BNM with full audit trails, and must integrate with the digital service delivery pipeline to ensure documentation is captured as a first-class deliverable. Access controls must prevent unauthorised modification while ensuring authorised staff can retrieve documents without delay.

requirements.requirement: Deploy and maintain a centralised Document Management System (DMS) or secure regulatory repository specifically catalogued for RMiT notification artefacts, with mandatory metadata tagging (clause reference, digital service ID, notification date, document type, version, and retention period) to enable rapid retrieval on BNM's request.
requirements.rationale: The institution cannot 'provide relevant documents' efficiently without a structured repository. A DMS with standardised metadata enables keyword and filter-based retrieval within minutes, not days, and ensures no documents are lost between system migrations.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (system availability); annual technology health review

requirements.requirement: Implement version control and immutable audit logging for all documents stored in the regulatory repository, ensuring that each document modification is timestamped, attributed to a named user, and that prior versions are preserved and retrievable, preventing retrospective alteration of submitted materials.
requirements.rationale: BNM's review of documents must be supported by evidence of authenticity and integrity. Immutable version history protects the institution from allegations of document manipulation and satisfies the evidentiary standards implicit in regulatory review under DFIA 2013.
requirements.frequency: Continuous; audit log integrity verified quarterly

requirements.requirement: Implement a secure, encrypted file-transfer mechanism (e.g., BNM-approved secure portal, PGP-encrypted email, or SFTP) for transmitting documents to BNM, with a chain-of-custody record capturing the transmission timestamp, file hash, recipient confirmation, and authorising officer identity for every document submission.
requirements.rationale: Providing documents 'for the Bank's review' requires a transmission channel that preserves confidentiality, integrity, and non-repudiation. Unsecured transmission (e.g., plain email) exposes sensitive operational and customer data to interception and undermines the evidentiary value of submissions.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each submission); channel security reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Integrate document-creation and upload tasks as mandatory steps within the institution's CI/CD pipeline and digital service delivery project management tooling, so that artefacts such as architecture diagrams, security test reports, and user acceptance testing (UAT) evidence are automatically linked to the corresponding notification record in the DMS upon completion.
requirements.rationale: Manual documentation at project close is unreliable and often incomplete. Automated pipeline integration ensures artefacts are captured in real time during development, guaranteeing the DMS contains up-to-date documents at the point of any BNM request.
requirements.owner: Head of Software Engineering / Head of DevSecOps
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each release pipeline execution)

requirements.requirement: Apply role-based access controls (RBAC) to the regulatory document repository, restricting write and delete permissions to document owners and system administrators, granting read-only access to compliance and legal staff, and enabling time-limited, access-logged guest retrieval tokens for BNM submission packages.
requirements.rationale: Documents submitted for BNM's review must be demonstrably unaltered and controlled. RBAC prevents unauthorised modification, while access-logged retrieval tokens create a defensible chain of custody evidencing exactly what was made available to the regulator.
requirements.frequency: Access rights reviewed semi-annually; enforced continuously

governance.summary: Governance structures must ensure that the obligation to provide documents to BNM is owned at the executive level, embedded in board-approved policy, and subject to ongoing monitoring so that the institution's readiness is a verifiable, auditable state rather than an assumed one. The Board and senior management bear accountability for ensuring that document production capability is maintained continuously, not activated only when a BNM request arrives. Governance frameworks must also assign clear cross-functional ownership across compliance, legal, technology, and business units to prevent accountability gaps.

requirements.requirement: Adopt a Board-approved Regulatory Document Management Policy that explicitly covers the obligation under RMiT 16.6, defining: document categories in scope, retention periods aligned with BNM and internal requirements, ownership accountability by role, response SLAs for BNM requests, and consequences of non-compliance for internal staff.
requirements.rationale: A 'shall provide' Standard obligation requires institutionalised governance, not ad-hoc compliance. Board approval elevates the policy to the highest governance level, ensuring resource allocation and executive accountability are formally mandated.
requirements.frequency: Policy adopted initially; reviewed and reaffirmed annually

requirements.requirement: Establish a cross-functional Regulatory Submission Working Group (comprising Compliance, Legal, Technology, and relevant Business Unit heads) with a standing mandate to coordinate responses to BNM document requests under this clause, chaired by the Chief Compliance Officer and reporting to the Management Risk Committee.
requirements.rationale: 'Relevant documents' for digital service reviews span multiple domains. Without a standing cross-functional body, each BNM request risks fragmented coordination, delayed escalation, and submission gaps. Formalising the working group ensures accountability is distributed but ultimately centrally directed.
requirements.frequency: Convened per-event (BNM request); standing terms reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Incorporate a 'document readiness' standing agenda item in the Management Risk Committee's quarterly reporting cycle, requiring Compliance to present the status of the regulatory document inventory, any pending or completed BNM requests under this clause, and the outcome of the most recent simulation exercise.
requirements.rationale: Governance oversight of this obligation must be continuous, not reactive. Quarterly Management Risk Committee visibility ensures senior management detects and remedies readiness gaps before a BNM request exposes them, supporting the Standard marker's expectation of sustained compliance.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer / Management Risk Committee Chair
requirements.frequency: Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Designate a named Document Compliance Owner for each notified digital service, accountable from the date of notification through the full regulatory retention period, with documented hand-off procedures to ensure continuity if the owner changes role or leaves the institution.
requirements.rationale: The obligation to 'provide relevant documents' persists beyond launch and may be triggered years after a service goes live. Without a named owner bound to the specific service lifecycle, institutional memory erodes and retrieval becomes dependent on tribal knowledge.
requirements.owner: Chief Digital Officer / relevant Business Unit Head
requirements.frequency: Assigned per-event; ownership registry reviewed semi-annually

requirements.requirement: Include RMiT 16.6 document-provision readiness as an explicit scope item in the institution's annual internal audit plan, with the internal audit function assessing: completeness of the document inventory, adequacy of the DMS controls, timeliness of historical BNM request responses, and alignment of the Regulatory Document Management Policy with current BNM guidance.
requirements.rationale: Internal audit provides independent assurance that governance controls are operating effectively. Without explicit audit coverage of this clause, the institution cannot provide the Board with credible assurance that the 'shall provide' obligation can be met on demand, particularly given the Standard (S) marker's binding force.
requirements.frequency: Annual (full audit); follow-up findings tracked quarterly

16.7.subsection: Introduction or enhancement to digital services
16.7.title: Immediate Offering Upon Notification

business.summary: Clause 16.7 grants financial institutions the right to launch or enhance digital services immediately upon notification submission and prior compliance — eliminating any BNM approval wait. The primary business risk is premature launch: commencing operations before notification is formally submitted or before all paragraphs 16.2–16.6 conditions are met exposes the institution to regulatory breach. A secondary risk is operational over-caution — building unnecessary internal waiting periods that slow time-to-market and undercut the intended facilitative nature of the notification regime.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal pre-launch gate that confirms: (1) the paragraph 16.1 notification has been submitted to BNM, and (2) all requirements under paragraphs 16.2 to 16.6 are demonstrably satisfied, before any digital service or enhancement goes live.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text makes the right to immediate launch conditional on both notification submission AND prior compliance with 16.2–16.6. Launching without meeting both conditions constitutes a regulatory breach, regardless of intent. A documented gate prevents premature activation.
requirements.owner: Chief Digital Officer / Head of Digital Banking
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each new digital service launch or material enhancement)

requirements.requirement: Remove any internally imposed BNM approval waiting periods from the digital service launch lifecycle, replacing them with a compliance-verification milestone tied to the notification submission date.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly states no waiting period or BNM approval is required post-submission. Retaining legacy approval-wait steps creates unnecessary time-to-market delays, competitive disadvantage, and misaligns internal process with the regulatory framework.
requirements.frequency: One-time process redesign; reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Maintain a timestamped submission record for every BNM paragraph 16.1 notification, which serves as the authoritative trigger point for business teams to commence live operations on digital services.
requirements.rationale: The right to offer services arises 'upon submission' — the submission timestamp is therefore a legally significant event. Business teams need a single authoritative record to determine when the right to launch has crystallised, avoiding disputes over whether launch preceded or followed submission.
requirements.owner: Head of Regulatory Affairs / Chief Compliance Officer
requirements.frequency: Per-event; records retained per BNM record-keeping requirements

requirements.requirement: Communicate the notification-based launch model to business and product stakeholders, so that product roadmaps, commercial commitments, and marketing campaigns are timed relative to notification submission rather than an anticipated BNM response date.
requirements.rationale: Misunderstanding the notification regime (e.g., assuming BNM must respond before launch) leads to commercial commitments and customer communications that are either prematurely made or unnecessarily delayed. Awareness prevents both operational and reputational harm.
requirements.owner: Chief Marketing Officer / Chief Product Officer
requirements.frequency: At onboarding of new product staff; annual refresh

technology.summary: Technology teams must build the operational infrastructure to verify, record, and evidence compliance with paragraphs 16.2–16.6 prior to any digital service activation, and must generate an immutable audit trail linking the BNM notification submission event to the service go-live event. Systems must enforce the pre-launch gate programmatically rather than relying solely on manual process. Additionally, deployment pipelines should be structured so that the notification submission timestamp is a required input before production release is permitted.

requirements.requirement: Implement a compliance checklist module within the digital service launch workflow that requires documented sign-off on each of paragraphs 16.2–16.6 before the production deployment pipeline can be initiated.
requirements.rationale: The clause conditions the right to immediate launch on compliance with 16.2–16.6. Encoding this as a system-enforced gate rather than a manual check ensures the condition cannot be bypassed under time pressure, and creates an auditable record of pre-launch compliance verification.
requirements.owner: Head of IT / Head of Software Engineering
requirements.frequency: Per-event; gate logic reviewed at least annually or upon BNM regulatory updates

requirements.requirement: Integrate the BNM notification submission event (including submission timestamp, reference number, and notifying officer) into the change management or release management system as a mandatory artefact linked to the digital service release record.
requirements.rationale: The right to launch arises 'upon submission' under paragraph 16.1. The technology audit trail must capture this causal link — that production activation followed, not preceded, the notification submission — so the institution can demonstrate compliance to BNM examiners.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Risk / Chief Information Officer
requirements.frequency: Per-event; artefact retained in release management system for the duration required by BNM record-keeping policy

requirements.requirement: Ensure that production deployment tooling captures the exact go-live timestamp for each digital service launch or enhancement, creating an immutable, correlated record pairing (notification submission datetime) → (service activation datetime) for regulatory audit purposes.
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating that services were offered only after notification submission requires tamper-evident timestamps on both events. Without this, the institution cannot prove the temporal sequence required by the clause, leaving it exposed during BNM examinations.
requirements.owner: Head of DevOps / Head of IT Operations
requirements.frequency: Automated per-event capture; log integrity reviewed quarterly

requirements.requirement: Configure the digital service environment (API gateways, feature flags, or deployment controls) so that new services or enhancements can be activated at will on short notice once the compliance gate is cleared, without requiring additional infrastructure provisioning that could impose a de facto waiting period.
requirements.rationale: The clause's facilitative intent — immediate offering — is undermined if technology infrastructure requires days of provisioning after notification submission. Pre-staging environments and feature-flag mechanisms preserve the institution's ability to launch on the same day as notification.
requirements.frequency: Architectural review annually; infrastructure readiness verified per-event pre-launch

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised digital services registry that records each notification-linked service, its compliance status against 16.2–16.6, notification submission date, and live activation date, accessible to Compliance, IT Risk, and Internal Audit teams.
requirements.rationale: A registry provides a consolidated view for oversight functions to verify that no service has gone live without meeting the clause's dual conditions, supports internal audit testing, and enables rapid evidence production during BNM supervisory reviews.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer / Head of IT Risk
requirements.frequency: Updated per-event; reviewed by Internal Audit at least annually

governance.summary: Governance structures must translate the notification-based launch right into enforceable internal policy, clear role assignments, and documented accountability chains. The Board and senior management must be satisfied that no digital service is activated outside the prescribed conditions, and that the absence of a BNM waiting period does not create a permissive culture that shortcuts the upstream requirements in 16.2–16.6. Internal audit and compliance monitoring must periodically verify that actual launch events conform to this framework.

requirements.requirement: Formalise a Digital Services Notification and Launch Policy that defines: the sequence of activities from 16.2–16.6 compliance verification through to paragraph 16.1 notification submission and live activation; roles responsible for each step; and the mandatory artefacts that must be produced and retained.
requirements.rationale: The clause's conditionality — immediate launch is permitted only after submission AND 16.2–16.6 compliance — must be operationalised in enforceable policy to prevent ad hoc or inconsistent application. Policy formalisation also demonstrates to BNM that the institution has embedded the notification framework into its governance structure.
requirements.frequency: Policy approved by Board Risk Committee; reviewed at least annually or upon BNM regulatory amendments

requirements.requirement: Designate a named Regulatory Notification Coordinator (or equivalent role) with sole authority to submit paragraph 16.1 notifications to BNM and to formally authorise the go-live signal to product and technology teams upon confirmed submission.
requirements.rationale: The clause ties the right to launch to the act of notification submission. Assigning a designated coordinator ensures submissions are not made prematurely, are properly documented, and that the authorisation to launch is controlled rather than assumed by individual teams independently.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer / Head of Regulatory Affairs
requirements.frequency: Role assignment reviewed annually; exercised per-event

requirements.requirement: Include a standing agenda item in the IT/Digital Risk Committee for review of all digital service launches or enhancements initiated under the notification regime in the preceding quarter, confirming post-hoc that each activation was preceded by valid notification submission and 16.2–16.6 compliance.
requirements.rationale: Governance oversight must not be limited to pre-launch approval alone. Periodic post-hoc review detects cases where the temporal sequence was not followed, identifies process gaps, and creates an accountability loop at senior management level consistent with BNM's risk governance expectations.
requirements.owner: Chief Risk Officer / Chair of IT Risk Committee
requirements.frequency: Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Incorporate testing of compliance with clause 16.7 into the Internal Audit plan, specifically verifying a sample of notification-linked launches to confirm: (a) notification was submitted before go-live, (b) 16.2–16.6 requirements were met and evidenced at the time of submission, and (c) records were retained in accordance with policy.
requirements.rationale: Independent assurance over the notification-to-launch sequence provides the Board with confidence that the facilitative right granted by clause 16.7 is not being exploited to circumvent upstream safeguards. This also prepares the institution for BNM thematic reviews on digital service governance.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor / Head of Internal Audit
requirements.frequency: At least annually; or triggered by a material digital service launch event

requirements.requirement: Ensure the Board Risk Committee receives an annual summary report on digital services launched under the notification regime, including the number of notifications submitted, any instances of non-compliance with pre-launch conditions, and management's remediation actions.
requirements.rationale: Board-level visibility of the notification regime's use and any failures to comply with pre-launch conditions is necessary for the Board to discharge its oversight responsibilities under BNM's corporate governance expectations. It also signals to BNM that senior leadership actively monitors adherence to the notification framework.
requirements.frequency: Annual; escalated immediately in cases of material non-compliance

17.1.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.1.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.1.title: First-Time Cloud and Emerging Technology Consultation

business.summary: This clause creates a mandatory gate before any first-time deployment of public cloud or emerging technology in critical systems, directly binding senior leadership accountability to the regulatory approval process. Failure to consult BNM prior to adoption exposes the institution to regulatory sanctions, potential forced rollback of critical infrastructure, and reputational damage. Business units sponsoring cloud or emerging technology programmes must build the pre-consultation process — including risk assessment, executive sign-off, and third-party review — into project timelines and budgets before any critical system migration proceeds.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal pre-adoption business case gate for any first-time use of public cloud or emerging technology in critical systems that mandates BNM consultation as a prerequisite to production deployment or contractual commitment with a cloud service provider.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly states the institution 'is required to consult the Bank prior to the first-time adoption' — proceeding without this gate risks non-compliance, regulatory censure, and potential forced decommissioning of already-deployed critical infrastructure, creating severe operational and financial disruption.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (each first-time adoption of a new public cloud platform or emerging technology for a critical system)

requirements.requirement: Commission a comprehensive risk assessment report for each proposed public cloud or emerging technology adoption for critical systems, formatted in accordance with Appendix 7 Part A, covering all risks specified in Appendix 10 (for public cloud) or Appendix 9 (for emerging technology), including impact analysis and mitigation measures.
requirements.rationale: Sub-clause (a) mandates this assessment as a precondition for BNM consultation. Without a complete, correctly formatted risk assessment, the institution cannot demonstrate to BNM's satisfaction that risks have been 'adequately considered and addressed', blocking the adoption pathway entirely.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each first-time cloud or emerging technology adoption for a critical system)

requirements.requirement: Procure and budget for an independent third-party pre-implementation review for every first-time public cloud or emerging technology adoption for critical systems, with scope extended to cover Appendix 7 Part C requirements for any service involving processing or storage of customer information or cross-border data transmission.
requirements.rationale: Sub-clause (c) mandates this third-party review as a non-negotiable pre-consultation step. Higher-risk services — specifically those handling customer data or involving cross-border transmission — trigger the extended Part C scope, and business sponsors must allocate sufficient procurement lead time and budget to avoid delaying the regulatory consultation timeline.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each first-time adoption; extended scope triggered by customer data or cross-border data characteristics)

requirements.requirement: Maintain an institutional register of all public cloud platforms and emerging technologies that have already undergone BNM first-time consultation and received approval for use in critical systems, to avoid redundant consultations and to identify genuinely novel adoptions that trigger the clause.
requirements.rationale: The clause applies specifically to 'first-time adoption', making it essential for business and IT leadership to track prior approvals. Without this register, the institution risks either unnecessarily repeating the consultation process for already-approved technologies or, more critically, failing to consult when a genuinely new platform or technology is introduced.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (maintained and reviewed at each new cloud or technology adoption decision point)

technology.summary: Technology teams are required to produce technically rigorous risk assessment artefacts aligned to BNM's prescribed appendices, support and facilitate the third-party pre-implementation review, and ensure that no critical system is migrated or connected to a public cloud or emerging technology platform until the full pre-consultation evidence package is complete. The clause's references to Appendix 10 (cloud-specific risk areas) and Appendix 9 (emerging technology guidelines) impose specific technical assessment domains — including data residency, encryption, access control, resilience, and exit strategy — that must be addressed with documented technical evidence before BNM consultation commences.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a structured cloud risk assessment methodology aligned to the risk domains in Appendix 10, covering at minimum: data sovereignty and residency, encryption in transit and at rest, access control and identity federation, resilience and recovery, vendor lock-in and exit strategy, and shared responsibility model gaps. Output must conform to Appendix 7 Part A format.
requirements.rationale: Sub-clause (a) requires the risk assessment to 'follow the risk outlined in paragraph 10.50 and Appendix 10' for public cloud, making Appendix 10 alignment a direct regulatory requirement. A methodology that omits prescribed risk domains will result in an incomplete Appendix 7 Part A submission, which BNM will reject as insufficient evidence of risk consideration.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (produced for each first-time cloud adoption); methodology reviewed annually or upon material change to BNM guidance

requirements.requirement: Define and document a technical classification framework to determine whether a proposed technology constitutes an 'emerging technology' under BNM's definition (referencing Appendix 9 criteria), and apply this classification at the start of every new technology adoption project for critical systems to determine if the clause 17.1 consultation pathway is triggered.
requirements.rationale: The clause applies equally to emerging technology alongside public cloud. Without a clear technical classification process, the institution may inadvertently deploy AI, distributed ledger, quantum, or other emerging technologies in critical systems without triggering the mandatory consultation, exposing the institution to non-compliance.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (applied at project initiation for every new technology involving critical systems)

requirements.requirement: Prepare a comprehensive technical evidence pack to support the third-party pre-implementation review required under sub-clause (c), including architecture diagrams, data flow mappings, security control documentation, API integration specifications, and resilience test results. For services involving customer data or cross-border transmission, ensure the evidence pack also addresses the additional scope areas in Appendix 7 Part C.
requirements.rationale: The independent third-party reviewer requires sufficient technical artefacts to assess readiness against Appendix 10 or Appendix 9 requirements. Incomplete documentation will result in a review that cannot substantively address BNM's prescribed scope, producing a Part C report that fails to satisfy the Bank during consultation.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (produced and updated prior to each third-party review engagement)

requirements.requirement: Implement a technical control gate in the organisation's cloud and infrastructure provisioning pipeline that prevents production deployment of any new public cloud environment or emerging technology for critical systems unless a completed BNM consultation record and approval artefact is present in the GRC system for that platform or technology.
requirements.rationale: Without a technical enforcement mechanism, project delivery pressure may result in teams bypassing the consultation gate. A provisioning control ensures the regulatory requirement is operationally enforced rather than relying solely on policy awareness, reducing the risk of inadvertent non-compliance during critical system deployments.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (enforced at every critical system cloud provisioning event)

requirements.requirement: Establish a data classification and flow mapping process to automatically identify whether any proposed cloud or emerging technology adoption involves the processing or storage of customer information or cross-border data transmission, to trigger the mandatory extended third-party review scope under Appendix 7 Part C.
requirements.rationale: Sub-clause (c) specifically elevates the third-party review requirements for 'higher-risk services, such as those involving the processing or storage of customer information, or cross-border data transmission'. Without a systematic data classification step, higher-risk services may be scoped under the standard review, producing an inadequate Part C submission and failing BNM's consultation requirements.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (applied during initial scoping of each cloud or emerging technology adoption project)

governance.summary: This clause establishes a board-and-senior-management-accountable governance process that must be completed, evidenced, and submitted to BNM before any first-time critical system deployment on public cloud or emerging technology can proceed. The requirement for a formal written confirmation from the CISO, a senior management officer, or the designated board-level committee chair directly embeds regulatory accountability at the highest level of the institution. Governance structures must ensure that the three pre-consultation artefacts — the Appendix 7 Part A risk assessment, the Part B executive confirmation, and the Part C third-party review — are produced in sequence, reviewed for completeness, and formally submitted to BNM with adequate lead time before any planned go-live date.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal BNM Pre-Consultation Governance Process documented in the institution's IT and cloud governance policy, specifying the mandatory sequence of activities (risk assessment → third-party review → executive confirmation → BNM consultation submission) and the governance body responsible for approving the submission package before it is transmitted to BNM.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the institution to 'undertake the following prior to consulting the Bank', imposing a prescribed sequence. Without a formalised process, the institution risks submitting incomplete or out-of-sequence artefacts to BNM, undermining the Bank's confidence that risks have been adequately addressed and potentially triggering a rejection or request for remediation before adoption can proceed.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed annually; process executed per-event for each first-time adoption

requirements.requirement: Obtain and retain a formal written confirmation of the institution's readiness to adopt public cloud or emerging technology for critical systems, signed by the CISO, a senior management officer, or the chairman of the board or designated board-level committee as specified in paragraph 8.3, formatted strictly in accordance with Appendix 7 Part B, prior to initiating BNM consultation.
requirements.rationale: Sub-clause (b) mandates this confirmation as a precondition for consultation and prescribes the specific signatories and format. This requirement directly assigns personal accountability to named senior individuals, making it a critical governance control. An unsigned or incorrectly formatted confirmation will invalidate the pre-consultation package submitted to BNM.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) / Board Risk Committee Chairman
requirements.frequency: Per-event (required for each first-time public cloud or emerging technology adoption for a critical system)

requirements.requirement: Appoint and formally engage an independent third-party reviewer with demonstrable competency in cloud security or emerging technology risk assessment, ensuring the review engagement letter explicitly scopes coverage to the applicable BNM appendix areas (Appendix 10 for cloud, Appendix 9 for emerging technology) and Appendix 7 Part C for higher-risk services, and that the final report is reviewed and accepted by governance prior to BNM submission.
requirements.rationale: Sub-clause (c) requires the third-party review to 'encompass the areas set out in Appendix 10 or Appendix 9 and Part C of Appendix 7'. Governance must ensure the reviewer's scope of work explicitly references BNM's prescribed areas; a review that omits prescribed scope areas or is conducted by a party lacking independence will not satisfy BNM's expectations during consultation.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each first-time adoption); vendor selection reviewed periodically to confirm ongoing independence and competency

requirements.requirement: Maintain a BNM Consultation Register documenting every pre-consultation submission made under clause 17.1, including the submission date, the technology or cloud platform subject to consultation, the BNM response or approval outcome, and all supporting artefacts (Appendix 7 Parts A, B, and C). This register must be accessible to internal audit and available for BNM examination.
requirements.rationale: While the clause does not explicitly require a register, BNM's supervisory expectations and prudent governance require the institution to demonstrate a complete audit trail of consultations. The register enables the institution to evidence compliance during regulatory examinations and to track which technologies have already received BNM approval, preventing inadvertent re-triggering of the consultation requirement for already-approved platforms.
requirements.owner: Company Secretary / Head of Regulatory Affairs
requirements.frequency: Continuous (register updated per-event; reviewed by internal audit annually)

requirements.requirement: Integrate the clause 17.1 pre-consultation requirements into the institution's IT project governance framework as a mandatory project phase gate, requiring sign-off from Risk, Compliance, and the relevant board committee before the project is permitted to proceed to the BNM submission phase, and ensuring project plans for critical system cloud or emerging technology migrations allocate sufficient time for risk assessment, third-party review, and BNM consultation response periods.
requirements.rationale: BNM's consultation process is not instantaneous — the Bank must be satisfied before the institution proceeds, and this satisfaction is at BNM's discretion. Without embedding this gate into project governance with realistic lead times, project teams may schedule go-live dates that are incompatible with the regulatory consultation timeline, forcing either non-compliant early deployment or costly project delays.
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Head of Project Management Office
requirements.frequency: Per-event (applied at project initiation and monitored at each project stage gate for critical system adoptions)

17.2.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.2.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.2.title: Subsequent Cloud and Emerging Technology Notification

business.summary: Clause 17.2 governs how a financial institution engages with BNM for every subsequent public cloud or emerging technology adoption affecting critical systems, shifting the burden from pre-approval consultation to a structured self-certified notification — but only after strict pre-conditions are satisfied. Failure to meet any pre-condition before notifying (rather than consulting) BNM exposes the institution to regulatory breach, potential enforcement action, and reputational risk. Business leaders must treat this as a lifecycle obligation triggered at each new critical-system cloud or emerging technology adoption event, not a one-off exercise.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a formal Pre-Condition Compliance Gate that must be passed before any subsequent public cloud or emerging technology notification is submitted to BNM. The gate must confirm: (a) the first-time consultation under 17.1 concluded with no concerns from BNM; (b) the technology risk management framework has been enhanced; (c) independent assurance has been obtained; and (d) incident response plan sufficiency has been assured to BNM.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly conditions the right to notify (rather than consult) on all four sub-paragraphs (a)–(d) being satisfied simultaneously. Submitting a notification without clearing every pre-condition invalidates the notification, constituting non-compliance with an 'S' marker standard.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each subsequent cloud or emerging technology adoption for a critical system)

requirements.requirement: Maintain a living regulatory correspondence register that records the outcome of every BNM consultation under paragraph 17.1, including formal confirmation that no concerns were raised. This register must be retrievable and included as evidence in every subsequent notification package submitted under 17.2.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (a) requires the institution to demonstrate that BNM raised no concerns during the first-time consultation. Without documented evidence of a concern-free outcome, the institution cannot substantiate eligibility to notify rather than consult for subsequent adoptions.
requirements.frequency: Continuous maintenance; retrieved per-event for each notification submission

requirements.requirement: For each subsequent cloud or emerging technology adoption for a critical system, compile and submit a complete notification package to BNM that includes all information and documentation required under paragraph 17.1, updated to reflect the new adoption, together with evidence of compliance with sub-paragraphs (a)–(d) of clause 17.2.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the notification to be submitted 'together with the necessary updates to all the information required under paragraph 17.1'. Submitting an incomplete or stale 17.1 information set alongside the 17.2 notification constitutes a deficiency in the submission and may trigger BNM follow-up or escalation.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (triggered by each subsequent critical-system cloud or emerging technology adoption decision)

requirements.requirement: Obtain and document formal business-level assurance — signed off by senior management — that incident response plans are sufficient to cater for adverse or unexpected events arising from cloud or emerging technology use, and include this assurance in every notification submitted to BNM under 17.2.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (d) requires the institution to have 'provided assurance to the Bank that the incident response plans are sufficient'. This is an active representation made to the regulator; it requires a deliberate, documented internal sign-off process before the assurance can be legitimately made.
requirements.frequency: Per-event; plans must also be reviewed annually or after any material incident

requirements.requirement: Integrate the clause 17.2 notification process into the institution's cloud and technology adoption lifecycle and project governance — no critical-system cloud or emerging technology adoption project may proceed to procurement or deployment without formal regulatory notification clearance under 17.2.
requirements.rationale: Without embedding the BNM notification obligation as a mandatory project gate, business units may inadvertently adopt public cloud or emerging technologies for critical systems before notification is submitted, creating a regulatory breach. This is particularly acute given the proliferation of SaaS, PaaS, and AI-based tools across business functions.
requirements.owner: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Chief Digital Officer (CDO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event; process reviewed annually

technology.summary: Technology teams bear direct responsibility for the two substantive technical pre-conditions in clause 17.2 — the enhancement of the technology risk management framework to address cloud and emerging technology risks (sub-paragraph b), and the sufficiency of incident response plans for adverse or unexpected cloud events (sub-paragraph d). In addition, technology must produce the complete technical evidence package required under paragraph 17.1 for each notification submission. These are not one-time deliverables; they must remain current and auditable for every subsequent adoption event.

requirements.requirement: Develop and implement a formally documented Cloud and Emerging Technology Risk Management Framework (CETRMF) as an enhancement to the institution's existing technology risk management framework. The CETRMF must address cloud-specific risks including shared responsibility model gaps, multi-tenancy, data residency, vendor lock-in, and emerging technology risks such as AI/ML model risk, quantum computing exposure, and API security. The framework must be versioned, with each enhancement documented and dated.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (b) mandates that the institution 'has enhanced the technology risk management framework to manage cloud or emerging technology risks'. This is an explicit technical artefact requirement — the enhanced framework must exist, be documented, and be demonstrably superior to the baseline framework predating cloud adoption. It is the foundation for independent assurance under sub-paragraph (c).
requirements.owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) / Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
requirements.frequency: Initial implementation prior to first-time consultation (17.1); reviewed and enhanced at each subsequent adoption event and at minimum annually

requirements.requirement: Develop cloud and emerging technology-specific incident response plans (IRPs) that extend the institution's enterprise IRP to address adverse and unexpected events unique to cloud environments — including cloud service provider (CSP) outages, data exfiltration via cloud APIs, misconfiguration incidents, sovereign data exposure, and AI model failure scenarios. IRPs must include defined recovery time objectives (RTO), recovery point objectives (RPO), BNM notification thresholds, and escalation matrices for CSP-involved incidents.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (d) requires the institution to provide BNM with 'assurance that the incident response plans are sufficient to cater for adverse or unexpected events'. Generic IRPs that do not address cloud-specific failure modes cannot substantiate this assurance. BNM's emphasis on 'adverse or unexpected events' implies scenarios beyond standard IT incidents.
requirements.frequency: Initial development prior to first cloud adoption; reviewed and tested at minimum annually and after each material incident or new technology adoption

requirements.requirement: Produce and maintain a complete technical evidence dossier aligned to all information requirements under paragraph 17.1, updated for each subsequent cloud or emerging technology adoption. This dossier must include current cloud architecture diagrams, data flow maps, risk assessments, vendor due diligence records, data residency documentation, and control mapping for each critical system adopted on cloud or emerging technology.
requirements.rationale: Clause 17.2 requires notifications to include 'necessary updates to all the information required under paragraph 17.1'. Technology teams must maintain living technical documentation for every critical cloud-hosted system so that notification packages can be assembled accurately and without delay at each adoption event.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (updated for each new critical-system adoption); reviewed at minimum annually

requirements.requirement: Implement continuous cloud security posture management (CSPM) tooling and risk monitoring capabilities that provide real-time visibility into cloud configuration compliance, threat indicators, and control effectiveness across all critical cloud-hosted systems. Monitoring outputs must feed into the CETRMF review cycle and be available as evidence for independent assurance engagements.
requirements.rationale: The requirement to have 'enhanced the technology risk management framework' under sub-paragraph (b) is not satisfied by documentation alone — controls must be operational and evidenced. CSPM tooling provides the ongoing technical assurance that risk controls are functioning, supporting both the framework requirement and the independent assurance requirement under sub-paragraph (c).
requirements.frequency: Continuous operation; findings reviewed monthly; full posture review at each new adoption event

requirements.requirement: Conduct tabletop exercises and technical simulation tests of cloud and emerging technology incident response plans at least annually and before each subsequent critical-system cloud adoption, producing documented test reports that demonstrate IRP sufficiency for adverse and unexpected events.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (d) requires assurance that IRPs are 'sufficient' — a claim that cannot be substantiated without evidence of testing. BNM expects tested and validated plans, not merely documented ones. Test reports also provide the objective evidence base for the independent assurance engagement required under sub-paragraph (c).
requirements.frequency: Annual minimum; additionally per-event before each subsequent cloud or emerging technology adoption for a critical system

governance.summary: Clause 17.2 imposes a structured self-certification regime on financial institutions: the institution must govern its own compliance with four specific pre-conditions and present that governance to BNM through each notification. This demands a robust governance architecture encompassing independent assurance over the technology risk framework, senior management accountability for incident response sufficiency, and a repeatable notification governance process embedded in the institution's change management lifecycle. Weak governance at any layer — particularly around independent assurance (sub-paragraph c) or senior management sign-off on incident response (sub-paragraph d) — directly undermines the regulatory validity of every subsequent notification.

requirements.requirement: Commission an independent assurance engagement — conducted by a qualified internal audit function, external auditor, or specialist third party with no involvement in designing the cloud risk management framework — on the Cloud and Emerging Technology Risk Management Framework (CETRMF). The assurance engagement must produce a formal assurance report confirming framework adequacy, and this report must be included in every subsequent notification package submitted under 17.2.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (c) requires the institution to have 'established independent assurance on the cloud risk management framework or emerging technology risk management framework'. The word 'independent' precludes self-assessment; it requires assurance from a party without a conflict of interest in the framework's design or operation. This is a hard pre-condition for notification eligibility.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor / Board Audit Committee Chairman
requirements.frequency: Initial engagement prior to first notification; refreshed at minimum every two years or after material changes to the framework or a new technology category is adopted

requirements.requirement: Establish a Cloud and Emerging Technology Adoption Governance Committee (or equivalent mandate within an existing risk committee) with authority to: (i) verify all four pre-conditions under clause 17.2 are satisfied before any notification is submitted; (ii) approve the content of notification packages to BNM; and (iii) maintain a forward-looking register of planned cloud and emerging technology adoptions for critical systems.
requirements.rationale: The clause's structured pre-condition architecture requires an institutional decision-making body to certify compliance before a notification is submitted. Without a designated governance forum, pre-condition verification becomes ad hoc, increasing the risk that a notification is submitted in breach of one or more conditions. BNM expects institutions to have mature governance for technology adoption decisions affecting critical systems.
requirements.frequency: Committee convenes per-event (prior to each notification submission) and at minimum quarterly to review the adoption pipeline

requirements.requirement: Implement a formal Board and Senior Management sign-off process for incident response plan sufficiency, resulting in a written attestation by the CEO or equivalent that cloud and emerging technology IRPs are adequate for adverse or unexpected events. This attestation must be included in each notification package submitted to BNM and retained in the institution's regulatory correspondence register.
requirements.rationale: Sub-paragraph (d) requires the institution to have 'provided assurance to the Bank' regarding IRP sufficiency — this is a representation made to a regulator at the most senior level. A written attestation from the CEO creates clear accountability and documents the governance chain behind the assurance, which is critical if BNM ever challenges IRP adequacy post-notification.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (for each notification submission); attestation reviewed annually or after material incidents or IRP revisions

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a Regulatory Notification Management Policy that defines: the triggering events requiring a 17.2 notification; the composition and ownership of the notification package; the pre-condition verification checklist aligned to sub-paragraphs (a)–(d); timelines for submission; and the regulatory correspondence record-keeping requirements for all BNM interactions under clauses 17.1 and 17.2.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires compliance with specific procedural conditions each time a subsequent cloud or emerging technology adoption occurs for a critical system. Without a governing policy, institutional knowledge of these obligations may be inconsistently applied across business units or may erode over time, particularly with staff turnover. The policy also supports auditability of the notification process.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed annually and updated upon any BNM guidance revision or institutional technology strategy change

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual compliance review assessing the continued adequacy of all clause 17.2 pre-conditions — even in periods where no new cloud or emerging technology adoption is planned — to ensure the institution remains in a perpetual state of notification readiness. Review outputs must be reported to the Board Risk Committee.
requirements.rationale: Pre-conditions under sub-paragraphs (b), (c), and (d) are ongoing obligations, not point-in-time achievements. The technology risk management framework may become outdated, independent assurance opinions may expire, and incident response plans may be invalidated by technology or operational changes. Annual review ensures the institution can credibly certify pre-condition compliance at any adoption event without remediation delays.
requirements.frequency: Annual; additionally triggered by material technology change, organisational restructuring, or BNM regulatory update

17.3.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.3.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.3.title: Enhancement Notification Exemption

business.summary: Clause 17.3 introduces a conditional exemption from BNM notification obligations, allowing financial institutions to make enhancements to cloud and emerging technology adoptions without triggering the Paragraph 17.2 notification process — provided those enhancements do not materially alter prior assessments or representations made to the Bank. Failure to correctly distinguish between exempt enhancements and notifiable changes exposes the institution to regulatory breach, reputational risk, and potential supervisory action. Conversely, over-notifying trivial changes creates operational friction; institutions must therefore maintain robust internal processes to manage this boundary precisely.

requirements.requirement: Define and formally adopt a Materiality Determination Framework that establishes explicit, quantified thresholds and qualitative criteria for determining whether an enhancement to an existing cloud or emerging technology adoption constitutes a 'material alteration' to prior BNM assessments and representations.
requirements.rationale: The clause's exemption hinges entirely on the concept of 'materially alter' — an undefined term that requires internal interpretation. Without a documented framework, business units will apply inconsistent judgement, creating both under-notification risk (regulatory breach) and over-notification burden (operational inefficiency). The framework must be grounded in the specific dimensions originally assessed and represented to BNM.
requirements.frequency: Established at policy inception; reviewed Annually or upon any BNM guidance update

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised, version-controlled Register of BNM Assessments and Representations that documents all prior assessments, commitments, and representations submitted to BNM for each approved cloud and emerging technology adoption, serving as the authoritative baseline against which proposed enhancements are evaluated.
requirements.rationale: The exemption is conditional on enhancements not materially altering 'prior assessments and representations made by a financial institution to the Bank.' Without a complete and accessible record of what was originally represented, there is no reliable baseline against which to compare proposed changes, making the exemption effectively unenforceable from a compliance standpoint.
requirements.frequency: Updated Per-event (on each new cloud/emerging tech adoption or BNM submission); reviewed Quarterly for completeness
