requirements.requirement: Implement a mandatory Pre-Enhancement Review process requiring all cloud and emerging technology change proposals to be screened against the BNM Assessment Register before implementation, with a documented materiality determination confirming either exemption eligibility or triggering the Paragraph 17.2 notification workflow.
requirements.rationale: The clause is self-executing in that the burden of determining exemption eligibility falls on the institution. A structured pre-implementation gate prevents business units from inadvertently proceeding with notifiable changes under the assumption of exemption, and creates an auditable decision trail demonstrating regulatory diligence.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (prior to each proposed enhancement)

requirements.requirement: Conduct targeted awareness and training programmes for technology project managers, product owners, and vendor management teams covering the scope of the Paragraph 17.3 exemption, the materiality framework, and the escalation pathway when exemption eligibility is uncertain.
requirements.rationale: Misapplication of the exemption typically occurs at the operational level where project teams assess scope changes. The verbatim text's distinction between exempt enhancements and notifiable material alterations is nuanced; frontline staff require practical guidance to apply it correctly without defaulting to legal counsel for every change.
requirements.owner: Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO) / Head of Compliance
requirements.frequency: Annual training with on-demand refreshers triggered by policy updates

technology.summary: Technology teams must build the infrastructure and tooling necessary to track approved cloud and emerging technology adoptions, capture the original assessment parameters represented to BNM, and systematically compare proposed enhancements against those baselines to support materiality determinations. Without automated change-tracking and baseline comparison capabilities, the institution cannot reliably invoke the Paragraph 17.3 exemption or demonstrate to BNM that exempted changes were genuinely non-material. Systems must also preserve immutable audit trails of all exemption decisions.

requirements.requirement: Deploy and maintain a Cloud and Emerging Technology Inventory System that captures, for each BNM-assessed adoption: the approved service scope, risk classification, deployment architecture, data residency parameters, vendor details, and all other dimensions explicitly represented to BNM — with version history tracking any subsequent changes to these parameters.
requirements.rationale: The exemption requires knowing precisely what was previously assessed and represented to BNM. A structured inventory system with versioned records ensures that when an enhancement is proposed, there is a reliable, queryable baseline to compare against rather than unstructured documentation spread across email or project files.
requirements.frequency: Continuously maintained; integrity audit Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Implement a Change Assessment Workflow integrated with the Cloud and Emerging Technology Inventory System that automatically surfaces the relevant BNM assessment record when a change request is raised for an existing cloud or emerging technology adoption, prompts the requestor to complete a structured materiality comparison, and routes the completed assessment for compliance sign-off before implementation approval.
requirements.rationale: The clause's exemption is conditional and per-event — it must be re-evaluated for each proposed enhancement. A workflow-enforced process prevents bypass, ensures consistent application of the materiality framework, and generates the evidence trail needed to demonstrate regulatory compliance if BNM queries an exempted change.
requirements.owner: Head of IT / Head of Technology Risk
requirements.frequency: Triggered Per-event; workflow SLA reviewed Quarterly

requirements.requirement: Establish tamper-evident, immutable audit logging for all materiality determinations made under Paragraph 17.3, capturing: the enhancement description, the baseline assessment record version referenced, the materiality determination outcome, the rationale, the approver identity, and the timestamp — retained for a minimum of seven years.
requirements.rationale: If BNM subsequently questions whether an exempted enhancement was in fact non-material, the institution must be able to produce contemporaneous evidence of the decision-making process. Immutable logs prevent post-hoc alteration and demonstrate the rigour of the institution's compliance programme.
requirements.frequency: Continuous logging; log integrity checks Quarterly; retention policy reviewed Annually

requirements.requirement: Configure automated alerting and escalation triggers within the change management system to flag enhancement proposals that approach or breach predefined materiality thresholds (as defined by the CRO's Materiality Determination Framework), routing these cases directly to the Compliance function for determination rather than allowing the project team to self-certify exemption.
requirements.rationale: The risk of the exemption being misapplied is highest at the margin — where changes are significant but arguably non-material. Automated threshold alerting removes the subjectivity from frontline project teams for borderline cases, ensuring that only genuinely low-risk enhancements are self-certified and that ambiguous cases receive expert compliance review.
requirements.frequency: Continuous monitoring; threshold calibration reviewed Annually or upon BNM guidance update

requirements.requirement: Integrate the Cloud and Emerging Technology Inventory System with the institution's broader IT Asset Management and Vendor Risk Management platforms to ensure that vendor-driven changes (e.g., cloud provider service updates, feature deprecations, architecture changes) are automatically ingested as candidate enhancement events and routed through the Paragraph 17.3 materiality screening workflow.
requirements.rationale: Material alterations can originate from the vendor side — a cloud provider retiring a feature or changing a data processing architecture may alter the parameters originally represented to BNM. Without automated ingestion of vendor change notifications, the institution's exemption controls apply only to self-initiated changes and will miss a significant class of potentially notifiable events.
requirements.owner: Head of IT / Head of Vendor Management
requirements.frequency: Continuous integration; feed reliability reviewed Quarterly

governance.summary: Governance structures must ensure that the institution's application of the Paragraph 17.3 exemption is policy-grounded, consistently applied, independently reviewed, and escalated appropriately when materiality is uncertain. The Board and senior management bear ultimate accountability for ensuring that the exemption is not used to circumvent the notification obligations of Paragraph 17.2, and must establish clear ownership, oversight, and periodic assurance mechanisms. The governance framework must also ensure that the Materiality Determination Framework evolves in step with changes in BNM's supervisory expectations and the institution's cloud adoption profile.

requirements.requirement: Formalise a Board-approved Cloud Enhancement Notification Policy that codifies the institution's interpretation of 'material alteration,' defines the governance process for applying the Paragraph 17.3 exemption, assigns accountability for materiality determinations, and establishes escalation pathways — including mandatory Board or Risk Committee notification for cases where exemption eligibility is disputed between technology and compliance functions.
requirements.rationale: The clause grants the institution discretion to determine what constitutes a non-material enhancement, but that discretion must be exercised within a documented, Board-endorsed framework to withstand supervisory scrutiny. Without a formal policy, exemption decisions are ad hoc and undefendable before BNM.
requirements.frequency: Policy approved at inception; reviewed Annually and upon any material change in BNM guidance or cloud strategy

requirements.requirement: Establish a Cloud Governance Committee (or extend the mandate of an existing Technology Risk Committee) with responsibility for reviewing and ratifying all materiality determinations made under Paragraph 17.3, maintaining oversight of the exemption register, and escalating to BNM any cases where post-hoc review reveals that an exempted enhancement may have in fact materially altered a prior BNM representation.
requirements.rationale: Centralised committee oversight prevents individual business units or project teams from applying the exemption unilaterally without adequate challenge. The requirement for voluntary escalation to BNM in cases of retrospective misclassification reflects the institution's good-faith obligation and is essential to maintaining the supervisory relationship.
requirements.frequency: Committee meets Quarterly at minimum; convenes ad hoc for material or borderline cases

requirements.requirement: Incorporate a dedicated review of Paragraph 17.3 exemption decisions into the institution's Internal Audit programme, with annual audits assessing: (a) completeness and accuracy of the BNM Assessment Register, (b) consistency of materiality determinations against the approved framework, (c) adequacy of audit trails, and (d) whether any exempted enhancements should have triggered notification under Paragraph 17.2.
requirements.rationale: Independent assurance is the primary mechanism for detecting systematic misapplication of the exemption before it becomes a regulatory issue. The audit scope must cover both process adequacy and substantive case-by-case review, as the regulatory risk arises from incorrect exemption decisions rather than procedural non-compliance alone.
requirements.owner: Head of Internal Audit / Audit Committee Chairman
requirements.frequency: Annual audit; findings tracked to closure on a Quarterly basis

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal Materiality Framework Review process triggered by defined events — including: issuance of new BNM guidance on cloud or emerging technology, significant expansion of the institution's cloud footprint, post-incident review following a BNM query on an exempted change, and scheduled Annual review — ensuring the framework's thresholds and criteria remain calibrated to current regulatory expectations.
requirements.rationale: The concept of materiality in the context of BNM's prior assessments is not static — it depends on what was originally represented and how the supervisory landscape has evolved. A governance-mandated review cycle ensures the institution's materiality standards do not become stale or misaligned with BNM's expectations over time.
requirements.frequency: Annual baseline review; event-triggered review Per-event

requirements.requirement: Maintain a consolidated Exemption Register — governed by the Compliance function — recording every enhancement for which the Paragraph 17.3 exemption was invoked, including the enhancement description, the materiality determination rationale, the approving authority, and a cross-reference to the specific BNM assessment record used as the baseline; this register must be produced to BNM upon request.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text frames the exemption as conditional on specific factual circumstances ('does not materially alter the prior assessments and representations'). If BNM exercises its supervisory power to investigate past enhancements, the institution must be able to demonstrate — with contemporaneous records — that each exempted change met the eligibility conditions. A centralised, compliance-owned register is the primary evidence artefact for this purpose.
requirements.frequency: Updated Per-event; reviewed for completeness Quarterly; presented to Cloud Governance Committee Quarterly

17.4.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.4.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.4.title: Bank Discretionary Direction

business.summary: Clause 17.4 grants BNM an unconditional override authority to impose consultation, notification, or Appendix 9/10 guidance obligations on any system — including those below criticality thresholds — at any time. Failure to respond promptly risks supervisory sanction, reputational damage, and potential licence conditions. Institutions must therefore maintain standing readiness across all technology tiers, not just systems already classified as critical.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a universal technology inventory covering all systems — critical and non-critical — that includes current cloud service model, deployment scope, associated vendor, and deviation status against Appendix 9 and Appendix 10 guidance, so that any BNM-directed consultation or notification can be fulfilled without delay.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly extends BNM's directive power to non-critical systems where the 17.1/17.2 pre-requisites have not been met. Without a complete inventory, the institution cannot demonstrate 'prompt' compliance when directed to consult or notify on a system not previously tracked for regulatory purposes.
requirements.frequency: Continuous — inventory must be maintained in real-time and reviewed quarterly for completeness

requirements.requirement: Define and publish an internally approved Service Level Obligation (SLO) for responding to a BNM discretionary directive under 17.4, specifying maximum elapsed time from directive receipt to: (a) acknowledgement to BNM, (b) submission of required consultation or notification package, and (c) deviation explanation if applicable.
requirements.rationale: The phrase 'promptly and to the satisfaction of the Bank' sets a performance standard without specifying a deadline. Institutions must operationalise 'prompt' internally to avoid ambiguity and demonstrate good-faith compliance. An undefined response window creates operational and regulatory risk.
requirements.frequency: Per-event — SLO triggered upon receipt of any BNM directive; SLO document reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Ensure all business units operating cloud services or emerging technologies — regardless of system criticality classification — are briefed on the possibility of BNM-directed obligations under 17.4, and are contractually and operationally capable of providing technical and business justification for deviations from Appendix 9 or Appendix 10 at short notice.
requirements.rationale: The clause's scope over non-critical systems means business units that have historically been exempt from 17.1/17.2 pre-requisites may unexpectedly receive a directive. Lack of business-unit preparedness would prevent prompt compliance and expose the institution to supervisory scrutiny.
requirements.frequency: Annual awareness and readiness exercise; immediate activation per BNM directive

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain documented deviation rationales for all instances where the institution has not fully observed Appendix 9 (cloud) or Appendix 10 (emerging technology) guidance, including for non-critical systems, so that explanations demanded under a 17.4 directive can be produced without requiring post-hoc reconstruction.
requirements.rationale: The clause specifically requires the institution to 'explain any deviations from the guidance in Appendix 10 or Appendix 9' when directed. Retrospectively constructing deviation rationales after a directive is received increases the risk of incomplete or inconsistent explanations that fail to satisfy BNM.
requirements.frequency: Per-event at point of deviation; reviewed semi-annually for currency and accuracy

technology.summary: To satisfy BNM's discretionary directive power, technology teams must implement systems capable of producing on-demand consultation packages, notification submissions, and deviation registers for any technology asset — including non-critical systems — with minimal lead time. The emphasis on 'prompt' compliance requires automation and pre-prepared artefacts rather than manual, ad-hoc assembly. Audit trails must also be maintained to evidence timely response.

requirements.requirement: Implement a centralised Technology Asset Register (TAR) that catalogues every cloud and emerging technology deployment across all criticality tiers, capturing: system name, criticality classification, cloud service model (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS), cloud service provider, deployment region, go-live date, Appendix 9/10 compliance status, and a link to any recorded deviation rationale. The TAR must be queryable and exportable within one business day.
requirements.rationale: BNM may direct consultation or notification for 'a non-critical system or when the pre-requisites in paragraphs 17.1 or 17.2 have not been met.' A TAR covering all tiers is the foundational data source required to respond promptly to any such directive without manual investigation.
requirements.frequency: Continuous update; quarterly reconciliation audit; annual system-of-record review

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain pre-populated consultation and notification templates aligned to paragraph 17.1 and 17.2 requirements for all material systems — including non-critical systems — so that when a 17.4 directive is received, the substantive content requires only final review and sign-off rather than drafting from scratch.
requirements.rationale: The obligation to comply 'promptly and to the satisfaction of the Bank' cannot be met if the institution must assemble a consultation or notification package from raw data after a directive is received. Pre-built templates reduce turnaround time and improve submission quality.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Risk and Governance
requirements.frequency: Templates maintained continuously; refreshed when system architecture changes or BNM guidance is updated

requirements.requirement: Implement a Directive Response Tracking System (DRTS) to log all BNM directives received under 17.4, record the institution's response actions with timestamps, track submission status, and generate management reports on open and closed directives. The system must support audit retrieval for a minimum of seven years.
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating 'prompt' compliance to BNM's satisfaction requires an evidence trail. The DRTS provides the institution with internal accountability, escalation triggers if response SLOs are at risk of breach, and a defensible audit record for BNM examination.
requirements.owner: Head of IT Risk and Governance
requirements.frequency: Continuous operation; log integrity reviewed quarterly; retention policy reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Maintain a living Deviation Register for Appendix 9 and Appendix 10 guidance, capturing each deviation, the system affected (including non-critical), the rationale accepted by internal governance, the risk treatment applied, and the approving authority. The register must be version-controlled and retrievable within one business day of a BNM directive.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the institution to 'explain any deviations from the guidance in Appendix 10 or Appendix 9' when directed. A pre-maintained register prevents the risk of incomplete or contradictory deviation explanations being submitted under time pressure.
requirements.frequency: Updated at point of every deviation decision; reviewed and attested semi-annually

requirements.requirement: Integrate the Technology Asset Register and Deviation Register with the institution's GRC (Governance, Risk and Compliance) platform to enable automated alerts when a non-critical system's compliance status changes, when a deviation is recorded without an approved rationale, or when a BNM directive response SLO is approaching breach threshold.
requirements.rationale: Manual tracking of compliance status across all systems — including non-critical — creates information gaps that would prevent prompt response to a 17.4 directive. Automated alerting ensures that responsible owners are notified in time to act before a directive is received or immediately upon receipt.
requirements.frequency: Continuous monitoring; alert configuration reviewed quarterly

governance.summary: Clause 17.4 requires an institutional governance posture that treats BNM's discretionary directive as an immediate, highest-priority compliance event applicable to any system tier. Governance structures must assign unambiguous accountability for directive receipt, escalation, and response; establish documented escalation pathways that function within the institution's 'prompt' SLO; and ensure Board-level visibility over all 17.4 directives received. The absence of pre-requisites triggering 17.1 or 17.2 obligations does not diminish the urgency or severity of a 17.4 directive.

requirements.requirement: Designate a named senior accountable owner — at minimum at Head of Department level — responsible for receiving, logging, escalating, and coordinating the institution's response to any BNM discretionary directive issued under 17.4. This designation must be documented in the institution's Regulatory Compliance Policy and updated whenever the role changes.
requirements.rationale: The clause imposes a personal compliance obligation on the financial institution to comply 'promptly and to the satisfaction of the Bank.' Without a named accountable owner, a directive may be misrouted or deprioritised, resulting in a delayed response that BNM deems unsatisfactory.
requirements.frequency: Role designation reviewed annually and upon organisational change; activated per-event

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal BNM Directive Response Procedure that covers: (a) receipt and logging of a 17.4 directive, (b) immediate escalation to CRO and CEO within two business hours, (c) convening of a cross-functional response team (Legal, Technology, Risk, Compliance), (d) production and internal approval of the required consultation, notification, or deviation explanation, and (e) submission to BNM with a cover letter confirming timely compliance.
requirements.rationale: Compliance 'to the satisfaction of the Bank' requires not merely a response but a well-structured, accurate, and timely submission. A documented procedure prevents ad-hoc responses that may omit required content or miss internal approval steps, both of which could result in BNM deeming the response unsatisfactory.
requirements.frequency: Procedure reviewed annually; tested via simulation exercise annually; executed per-event

requirements.requirement: Extend the institution's Technology Risk Governance Framework to explicitly cover non-critical systems and scenarios where 17.1/17.2 pre-requisites have not been met, ensuring that risk acceptance, deviation approval, and cloud adoption decisions for non-critical systems are documented with the same rigour applied to critical systems, so that governance artefacts are available if BNM directs compliance under 17.4.
requirements.rationale: The clause expressly includes non-critical systems and waived pre-requisite scenarios within BNM's directive scope. Governance frameworks that only impose rigorous documentation requirements on critical systems will leave the institution unable to explain decisions for non-critical systems when directed.
requirements.frequency: Framework reviewed annually; governance artefacts maintained continuously per system lifecycle

requirements.requirement: Require Board Risk Committee (BRC) or Board-level reporting for all BNM directives received under 17.4, including the nature of the directive, systems affected, response submitted, and BNM's assessment of the institution's compliance. The BRC must be informed within five business days of directive receipt and within five business days of BNM's response.
requirements.rationale: A BNM discretionary directive signals heightened supervisory scrutiny and may foreshadow formal enforcement action if the institution's response is unsatisfactory. Board-level visibility ensures that the institution's most senior governance body is aware of and can oversee the adequacy of the response, consistent with the Board's overall accountability for regulatory compliance.
requirements.frequency: Per-event reporting; standing BRC agenda item for open directives; annual review of directive history

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual tabletop exercise simulating receipt of a BNM 17.4 directive for a non-critical system that has not previously been subject to 17.1 consultation or 17.2 notification, to validate the adequacy of the response procedure, the completeness of the Technology Asset Register and Deviation Register, and the institution's ability to produce a satisfactory submission within its defined SLO.
requirements.rationale: The clause's scope over non-critical systems and waived pre-requisite scenarios represents the highest-risk compliance gap — these are precisely the systems least likely to have pre-prepared documentation. Annual simulation testing identifies gaps before BNM exercises its directive power, reducing the risk of a real directive exposing inadequate readiness.
requirements.frequency: Annual; findings reported to Board Risk Committee

17.5.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.5.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.5.title: Cloud and Emerging Technology Roadmap

business.summary: Financial institutions must proactively plan and disclose their cloud and emerging technology adoption strategies to BNM through the annual outsourcing plan, covering all systems regardless of criticality. Failure to maintain a documented roadmap and accompanying risk assessments exposes the institution to regulatory non-compliance findings, potential supervisory intervention, and reputational damage. This clause requires senior leadership to treat technology adoption planning as a regulatory obligation, not merely an internal strategic exercise.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain an annual cloud and emerging technology adoption roadmap that explicitly covers both critical and non-critical systems, and incorporate this roadmap into the annual outsourcing plan submitted to BNM.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text states the institution 'must ensure the roadmap for adoption of cloud services or emerging technology (for critical systems and non-critical systems) is included in the annual outsourcing plan submitted to the Bank.' Omitting either system tier or failing to submit within the annual cycle constitutes a direct regulatory breach.
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Align the cloud and emerging technology roadmap with BNM's Outsourcing policy document requirements or IT Profile reporting format, ensuring all mandatory disclosures and classifications are met before submission.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates adherence 'with the requirements in the policy document on Outsourcing or IT Profile reporting.' Misalignment with the applicable reporting framework risks the submission being deemed non-compliant or incomplete by BNM supervisors.
requirements.frequency: Annual, prior to submission deadline

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal business planning process that links technology adoption decisions (cloud onboarding, emerging technology pilots) to the annual outsourcing plan cycle, ensuring no cloud or emerging technology initiative proceeds without roadmap inclusion.
requirements.rationale: The obligation to include the roadmap 'in the annual outsourcing plan' implies that ad-hoc or unplanned adoptions that bypass the annual disclosure mechanism are non-compliant. Business units sponsoring technology adoption must route approvals through the outsourcing plan process.
requirements.frequency: Continuous, formalised annually

requirements.requirement: Maintain on-demand readiness to present documented risk assessments for cloud services (per paragraph 10.50) and emerging technology (per Appendix 9) to BNM, with no lead time dependency on internal compilation activities.
requirements.rationale: The clause states risk assessments 'must be documented and made available for the Bank's review as and when requested.' The phrase 'as and when' implies zero-notice availability; institutions that require compilation time upon request are operationally non-compliant with the spirit of the obligation.
requirements.frequency: Continuous (on-demand upon BNM request)

technology.summary: Technology teams must maintain structured, version-controlled roadmap documentation for cloud and emerging technology initiatives across all system tiers, integrated into the institution's enterprise architecture and outsourcing governance tooling. Risk assessment artefacts mandated under paragraph 10.50 and Appendix 9 must be produced, maintained, and retrievable at any point in response to BNM examination requests. Systems and processes must support annual reporting cycles aligned to BNM's Outsourcing policy and IT Profile reporting formats.

requirements.requirement: Implement a centralised cloud and emerging technology roadmap registry that catalogues all current, planned, and evaluated cloud services and emerging technologies across critical and non-critical system classifications, with version history and annual snapshot capability.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires the roadmap to be 'included in the annual outsourcing plan,' implying it must be a structured, submittable artefact. A centralised registry ensures completeness across both system tiers ('critical systems and non-critical systems') and prevents shadow IT adoption from escaping disclosure.
requirements.frequency: Continuous maintenance; annual snapshot for submission

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain standardised risk assessment templates and completed artefacts for all cloud services in scope, aligned to the specific requirements of paragraph 10.50, stored in a document management system with access controls and audit trails.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates that 'the risk assessment as outlined in paragraph 10.50 for cloud services must also be documented and made available for the Bank's review.' Paragraph 10.50 prescribes specific risk dimensions; ad-hoc or non-standardised assessments will not satisfy BNM reviewers during examinations.
requirements.frequency: Per cloud service onboarding; reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain standardised risk assessment artefacts for all emerging technology initiatives in scope, aligned to the specific requirements of Appendix 9, stored alongside the roadmap registry with retrieval capability within one business day of BNM request.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly references 'Appendix 9 for emerging technology' as the required risk assessment framework. Emerging technology assessments must be distinct from cloud assessments, traceable to specific Appendix 9 criteria, and immediately accessible to support 'as and when requested' BNM review.
requirements.frequency: Per emerging technology initiative; reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Integrate the cloud and emerging technology roadmap and risk assessment output into the annual IT Profile reporting or outsourcing plan preparation workflow, with automated data feeds or structured export formats compatible with BNM's required submission templates.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates adherence 'with the requirements in the policy document on Outsourcing or IT Profile reporting.' Technical integration between internal roadmap tooling and submission workflows reduces manual compilation errors and ensures the submitted plan accurately reflects the institution's technology posture.
requirements.frequency: Annual, aligned to BNM submission cycle

requirements.requirement: Implement system classification tagging (critical vs. non-critical) for all cloud-hosted and emerging technology-enabled systems within the institution's configuration management database (CMDB) or equivalent asset inventory, ensuring the roadmap accurately reflects the regulatory scope.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly requires roadmap coverage of both 'critical systems and non-critical systems.' Without accurate system criticality classification in the asset inventory, the roadmap cannot reliably demonstrate full scope compliance to BNM examiners.
requirements.frequency: Continuous; reviewed as part of annual CMDB reconciliation

governance.summary: Governance structures must ensure the cloud and emerging technology roadmap is subject to formal board or senior management oversight, reviewed and approved before annual outsourcing plan submission to BNM. Standing policies and procedures must define ownership, review cadences, and escalation paths for both roadmap maintenance and risk assessment production. Audit and assurance functions must periodically validate that risk assessments are complete, current, and BNM-examination-ready.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board-level or Board IT Committee approval process for the annual cloud and emerging technology roadmap prior to its incorporation into the outsourcing plan submitted to BNM, with formal minuted resolutions evidencing oversight.
requirements.rationale: The clause places the obligation on 'a financial institution,' which in governance terms implies institutional accountability at the highest level. Board approval creates an auditable chain of accountability for the roadmap's completeness and accuracy as submitted to the regulator.
requirements.owner: Board Chairman / Board IT Committee Chairman
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Define and maintain a written policy governing the cloud and emerging technology roadmap process, specifying roles responsible for drafting, reviewing, and approving the roadmap, timelines aligned to the annual outsourcing plan cycle, and criteria for classifying systems as critical or non-critical.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires that roadmap inclusion in the outsourcing plan occurs 'in adherence with the requirements in the policy document on Outsourcing or IT Profile reporting.' A governing internal policy ensures the institution's process is documented, repeatable, and auditable against BNM's external policy requirements.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed annually; process executed annually

requirements.requirement: Assign named accountability for the production, maintenance, and regulatory readiness of cloud services risk assessments (paragraph 10.50) and emerging technology risk assessments (Appendix 9) to specific senior officers, documented in a RACI matrix or equivalent governance artefact.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates that risk assessments 'must be documented and made available for the Bank's review as and when requested.' Without explicit ownership, accountability gaps will result in incomplete or stale assessments that cannot be produced promptly upon BNM examination request.
requirements.frequency: Ownership confirmed annually; accountability continuous

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual internal audit or second-line assurance reviews of the cloud and emerging technology roadmap, associated risk assessments, and their alignment to the submitted outsourcing plan, with findings reported to the Audit Committee.
requirements.rationale: The 'as and when requested' availability requirement implies the institution cannot discover gaps only upon BNM examination. Independent assurance provides proactive verification that risk assessments exist, are current, and are aligned to the roadmap submitted to BNM, reducing supervisory risk.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor / Head of Compliance
requirements.frequency: Annual

requirements.requirement: Establish a regulatory response protocol specifically for BNM requests under this clause, defining maximum response timelines, designated custodians for risk assessment artefacts, and an escalation chain to senior management when requests are received.
requirements.rationale: The clause's phrase 'as and when requested by the Bank' places an implicit obligation of prompt and complete response. A pre-established protocol prevents disorganised or delayed responses during BNM examinations or supervisory reviews, which could themselves constitute a compliance finding.
requirements.owner: Head of Regulatory Affairs / Company Secretary
requirements.frequency: Protocol reviewed annually; activated on-demand

business.summary: This clause requires financial institutions to formally benchmark their technology risk management practices against BNM RMiT requirements and submit a remediation roadmap to the regulator within a strict 90-day window. Failure to comply exposes the institution to supervisory action, reputational damage, and potential enforcement under the Financial Services Act 2013 or Islamic Financial Services Act 2013. Institutions with prior submissions face an ongoing obligation to identify and close gaps introduced by revised or enhanced requirements, making compliance a continuous operational discipline rather than a one-time exercise.

requirements.requirement: Commission and complete a formal gap analysis comparing all existing technology risk management practices — including policies, procedures, controls, and organisational structures — against every requirement in the current RMiT policy document, with explicit identification of key implementation gaps.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text mandates that the institution 'perform a gap analysis of existing practices in managing technology risk against the requirements in this policy document and highlight key implementation gaps.' Without a comprehensive, documented baseline assessment, the institution cannot demonstrate regulatory compliance and risks submitting an incomplete or inaccurate gap report to BNM.
requirements.frequency: Once upon each new or revised issuance of the RMiT policy document; annually thereafter for continuous compliance

requirements.requirement: Develop a written action plan that maps each identified gap to a specific remediation activity, assigns clear accountability, and sets defined timelines with measurable key milestones for closure.
requirements.rationale: The clause explicitly requires 'an action plan with a clear timeline and key milestones to address the gaps identified.' A vague or incomplete plan without owners and dates cannot satisfy the regulatory obligation and will be insufficient if submitted to or reviewed by BNM.
requirements.frequency: Once per gap analysis cycle; updated whenever new gaps are identified through continuous compliance monitoring

requirements.requirement: Ensure the completed gap analysis and action plan are formally submitted to Bank Negara Malaysia no later than 90 calendar days from the official issuance date of the current RMiT policy document.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text states the submission 'must be submitted to the Bank no later than 90 days after the issuance date.' Missing this deadline constitutes a direct regulatory breach and may trigger supervisory escalation, making deadline tracking a critical business control.
requirements.frequency: Once per new policy issuance; submission deadline is fixed at 90 days from issuance

requirements.requirement: For institutions that previously submitted under an earlier version of RMiT, establish an ongoing programme to identify any new or widened gaps arising from enhanced or revised requirements in the latest policy version and take documented steps to address them.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires previously-submitting institutions to 'maintain continuous compliance by identifying any new gaps against the enhanced or revised requirements.' This creates a perpetual obligation that must be embedded in regular business operations rather than treated as a discrete project.
requirements.frequency: Continuous; triggered by each new or revised RMiT issuance and validated at minimum annually

requirements.requirement: Produce and maintain an updated annual assessment of the institution's level of compliance with RMiT requirements, in a format and level of detail sufficient to be presented to BNM upon request without further preparation.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text requires that 'the updated annual assessment of its level of compliance must be made available to the Bank upon request.' The assessment must be current and readily producible; an outdated or incomplete document presented on demand would indicate a failure of ongoing compliance management.
requirements.frequency: Annual; maintained as a living document updated whenever material changes to compliance status occur

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement structured tooling and processes to systematically catalogue all RMiT requirements, map them to existing controls, and track remediation progress against defined milestones. The obligation to maintain continuous compliance and produce an annual assessment on demand requires automated or semi-automated monitoring capabilities rather than reliance on ad-hoc manual reviews. Version-controlled artefacts and audit trails are essential to demonstrate to BNM that gaps have been identified and closed in an evidenced, repeatable manner.

requirements.requirement: Implement a structured RMiT requirements register that enumerates every clause and sub-clause of the policy document, with fields to record the current control status (compliant, partially compliant, non-compliant), evidence artefacts, and gap descriptions for each requirement.
requirements.rationale: A systematic, clause-by-clause register is the technical foundation for the gap analysis mandated in the verbatim text. Without it, the institution cannot reliably 'highlight key implementation gaps' or demonstrate completeness of the assessment to BNM.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer (CIO) / Head of IT Governance
requirements.frequency: Built once; reviewed and updated annually and upon each new RMiT issuance

requirements.requirement: Deploy an action plan tracking system — integrated with or supplementing the requirements register — that records each remediation task, assigned owner, planned completion date, key milestones, actual completion date, and closure evidence, with automated escalation alerts for approaching or missed deadlines.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim requirement for 'a clear timeline and key milestones' necessitates tooling that enforces accountability and makes milestone status visible to governance stakeholders. Automated alerts are necessary to meet the strict 90-day submission deadline and subsequent remediation commitments.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer (CIO) / Head of Technology Risk
requirements.frequency: Operational continuously; milestone status reviewed at minimum monthly by assigned owners

requirements.requirement: Establish a policy change management process supported by version-controlled documentation that tracks each revision to BNM RMiT, compares new or enhanced requirements against the previous version, and triggers a delta gap analysis workflow whenever a new version is issued.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires previously-submitting institutions to identify 'any new gaps against the enhanced or revised requirements in the latest version.' Without a structured mechanism to detect and analyse policy changes, new compliance obligations can be missed, exposing the institution to undetected regulatory breaches.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) / Head of IT Governance
requirements.frequency: Triggered upon each new RMiT issuance; delta analysis completed within 30 days to allow time for submission preparation

requirements.requirement: Implement a compliance evidence repository with access controls and audit logging, where all gap analysis working papers, supporting documentation, and remediation evidence artefacts are stored, version-controlled, and retrievable within 24 hours of a BNM request.
requirements.rationale: The clause stipulates the annual compliance assessment 'must be made available to the Bank upon request,' implying the institution must be able to produce it promptly. A well-organised, searchable evidence repository reduces retrieval risk and demonstrates documentary rigour during supervisory reviews.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) / Head of IT Governance
requirements.frequency: Maintained continuously; access and integrity reviewed quarterly

requirements.requirement: Develop a compliance dashboard reporting capability that presents real-time or near-real-time compliance status by RMiT section, gap closure trend, and action plan milestone progress, consumable by senior management and the Board Risk Committee.
requirements.rationale: Ongoing visibility into compliance posture is essential for the institution to 'maintain continuous compliance' as required by the clause. A dashboard enables timely escalation of stalled remediation items and provides the data foundation for producing the annual assessment for BNM.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer (CIO) / Head of Technology Risk
requirements.frequency: Dashboard maintained continuously; reports generated for governance committees on a monthly or quarterly basis

governance.summary: Clause 18.1 imposes direct accountability on the Board and senior management to oversee the gap analysis, approve the action plan, and ensure its timely submission to BNM — obligations that must be embedded in formal governance structures with clear terms of reference and escalation paths. The continuous compliance obligation for previously-submitting institutions requires governance frameworks that treat RMiT compliance as a standing agenda item rather than a periodic project. Board-level sign-off on the annual compliance assessment is necessary to demonstrate that the institution's highest decision-making body is aware of and accountable for its technology risk posture.

requirements.requirement: Establish a cross-functional RMiT Compliance Working Group with a defined Terms of Reference, chaired by the CRO, comprising representatives from Technology, Legal, Compliance, Internal Audit, and relevant business lines, with a mandate to own the gap analysis and action plan lifecycle.
requirements.rationale: The breadth of the gap analysis — covering all technology risk management practices across the institution — requires coordinated input from multiple functions. A formalised working group with clear accountability ensures the process is thorough, consistent, and completed within the 90-day deadline specified in the verbatim text.
requirements.frequency: Working Group constituted upon each new RMiT issuance; meets at minimum monthly during gap analysis and remediation phases, quarterly thereafter

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board-approved RMiT compliance policy that mandates the gap analysis methodology, assigns executive ownership of each RMiT section, defines the escalation and sign-off process for the action plan, and sets the 90-day submission as a hard internal deadline with a buffer of at least 10 business days for Board review prior to submission.
requirements.rationale: Without a governing policy, the gap analysis risks being conducted inconsistently, with unclear accountability. Board approval of the compliance policy anchors institutional commitment to the regulatory obligation and ensures the action plan receives appropriate executive endorsement before submission to BNM.
requirements.owner: Board Risk Committee / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed and reaffirmed annually and upon each new RMiT issuance

requirements.requirement: Implement a formal BNM submission governance process, including a preparation checklist, completeness review by Compliance, legal review for regulatory sensitivity, and written approval from the CEO prior to submission, with a documented submission record retained for audit purposes.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text mandates submission to BNM within 90 days, making the act of submission itself a regulated event that requires controlled, auditable governance. A formal process prevents premature, incomplete, or unauthorised submissions and demonstrates procedural discipline if BNM requests evidence of the submission process.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) / Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
requirements.frequency: Executed for each submission to BNM; process reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Mandate an annual RMiT compliance self-assessment exercise, culminating in a Board Risk Committee-reviewed compliance assessment report that documents the institution's current compliance level against each RMiT requirement, outstanding gaps, remediation status, and any new gaps identified since the previous assessment.
requirements.rationale: The clause requires 'the updated annual assessment of its level of compliance' to be available to BNM upon request. Embedding this as a Board-level governance activity — rather than a purely operational exercise — ensures senior accountability and produces a document of sufficient authority and completeness to satisfy regulatory scrutiny.
requirements.owner: Board Risk Committee / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Annual; timing aligned with the institution's risk governance calendar

requirements.requirement: Integrate RMiT gap closure progress and compliance status as standing agenda items in the Board Risk Committee and senior management technology risk governance forums, with escalation triggers defined for gaps that exceed planned remediation timelines or where new material gaps are identified.
requirements.rationale: The obligation to 'maintain continuous compliance' implies sustained oversight, not just point-in-time reviews. Embedding RMiT compliance into standing governance agendas ensures the Board and senior management receive timely information about compliance posture and can direct resources to address emerging gaps, consistent with the clause's continuous compliance expectation.
requirements.owner: Board Risk Committee Chairman / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Standing agenda item at each Board Risk Committee meeting (minimum quarterly); escalation triggered within 5 business days of a material compliance event

business.summary: This clause establishes a mandatory regulatory submission obligation, requiring institutions to formally deliver their self-assessment, gap analysis, and action plan to the correct BNM supervisory department. Failure to submit — or submitting to the wrong department — constitutes a direct breach of the RMiT standard, exposing the institution to supervisory scrutiny, enforcement action, and reputational risk. Business lines and senior leadership must ensure the submission package is complete, accurate, and routed correctly based on the institution's regulatory classification.

requirements.requirement: The institution must formally submit all three documents — the self-assessment, gap analysis, and action plan prepared under paragraph 18.1 — as a complete package to BNM. Partial submissions or submission of individual components separately are not compliant. The submission must not be considered complete until all three are delivered.
requirements.rationale: The verbatim text explicitly names all three artefacts ('self-assessment, gap analysis and action plan') as a single submission obligation. Submitting only one or two components would create a compliance gap and may trigger regulatory findings during supervision reviews.
requirements.frequency: Per-event (aligned with the assessment cycle defined under paragraph 18.1, typically annual or upon regulatory request)

requirements.requirement: The institution must formally identify and document which BNM supervisory department is responsible for its oversight — Jabatan Penyeliaan Konglomerat Kewangan, Jabatan Penyeliaan Perbankan, Jabatan Penyeliaan Insurans dan Takaful, or Jabatan Pemantauan Perkhidmatan Pembayaran — and ensure all submissions are routed exclusively to that department. This determination must be reviewed if the institution's regulatory status changes.
requirements.rationale: The clause's phrase 'as the case may be' imposes an institution-specific routing obligation. Submitting to the incorrect supervisory department could result in the submission being disregarded, delayed, or treated as non-compliant, with supervisory consequences including formal notices.
requirements.frequency: Annual review of department mapping; immediate update upon any change in regulatory classification or entity structure

requirements.requirement: The institution must establish and maintain an internal submission deadline calendar that precedes BNM's expected receipt date, allowing sufficient time for internal review, senior management endorsement, and document packaging before the regulatory submission is made.
requirements.rationale: Late submission to the supervisory department is a direct breach of the Standard ('S') marker obligation. An internal deadline buffer ensures that administrative delays, public holidays, or last-minute review cycles do not result in a missed submission.
requirements.frequency: Annual planning; tracked on a per-submission-cycle basis

requirements.requirement: The institution must obtain and retain formal acknowledgement of receipt from the relevant BNM supervisory department for each submission made under paragraph 18.2, and escalate immediately if acknowledgement is not received within a defined period.
requirements.rationale: Without proof of receipt, the institution cannot demonstrate that the submission obligation was fulfilled. In the event of a supervisory review or dispute, the absence of a delivery record is operationally equivalent to non-submission.
requirements.owner: Head of Regulatory Affairs / Chief Compliance Officer (CCO)
requirements.frequency: Per-submission; escalation SLA to be defined in internal policy (e.g., within 5 business days of submission)

requirements.requirement: For financial conglomerates or institutions operating across multiple licensed entities, the submission strategy must explicitly address whether each licensed entity submits independently to its respective supervisory department or whether a consolidated submission is made through the group-level entity, with clear accountability assigned for each submission.
requirements.rationale: Financial conglomerates may be supervised by multiple BNM departments across their subsidiaries. A failure to submit for any individual licensed entity — even if the parent submits — would constitute non-compliance for that entity. The phrase 'as the case may be' applies at the entity level.
requirements.owner: Group Chief Risk Officer / Chief Compliance Officer (CCO)
requirements.frequency: Per-event; entity mapping to be reviewed annually or upon group structural changes

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement secure, auditable workflows to support the preparation, packaging, and formal delivery of the three-document submission package to the correct BNM supervisory department. Systems must maintain tamper-evident records of submission artefacts, delivery timestamps, and BNM acknowledgements to provide evidence of compliance during supervisory reviews. Integration with the institution's GRC (Governance, Risk and Compliance) platform is strongly recommended to automate submission tracking and escalation.

requirements.requirement: Implement a secure document management repository that stores the finalised versions of the self-assessment, gap analysis, and action plan under version control, with access restricted to authorised personnel. The system must enforce document locking upon submission approval to prevent post-submission modification of the submitted artefacts.
requirements.rationale: The submitted documents must reflect a point-in-time assessment. If documents are modified after submission, the institution cannot demonstrate to BNM that what was submitted is what was reviewed and approved. Tamper-evidence is critical for regulatory defensibility.
requirements.owner: Chief Information Officer (CIO) / Head of IT Governance
requirements.frequency: Continuous availability; version snapshots captured per submission cycle

requirements.requirement: Implement a submission tracking module within the GRC platform that records the target BNM supervisory department per entity, submission date, method of delivery (e.g., BNM regulatory portal, secure email, physical submission), reference number, and acknowledgement receipt status. The system must generate alerts when acknowledgement is not received within the defined SLA.
requirements.rationale: The clause mandates submission to a specific department 'as the case may be', requiring a system of record that maps each institutional entity to its correct supervisory department and tracks whether the submission was successfully delivered and acknowledged.
requirements.owner: Head of IT / GRC Platform Administrator
requirements.frequency: Per-submission event; acknowledgement tracking continuous until confirmed

requirements.requirement: Establish an automated workflow within the GRC or document management system that enforces a mandatory pre-submission approval gate — requiring sign-off from designated approvers (e.g., CRO, CCO, CEO or Board Risk Committee) before the submission package can be released for delivery to BNM. The workflow must log all approval actions with timestamps and approver identities.
requirements.rationale: Submission to BNM is a high-consequence regulatory act. An automated approval gate ensures the correct authority has reviewed and endorsed the package before it leaves the institution, creating an auditable internal governance trail that demonstrates due diligence.
requirements.frequency: Per-submission event; workflow design reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive audit log of all activities related to the submission process, including document preparation, review comments, approval actions, submission dispatch, and acknowledgement receipt. Audit logs must be immutable, timestamped, and retained for a minimum period consistent with BNM's record retention requirements (typically seven years).
requirements.rationale: BNM supervisory departments may request evidence of compliance during on-site examinations or off-site reviews. Immutable audit logs provide the evidentiary chain demonstrating that the institution fulfilled its submission obligation under paragraph 18.2.
requirements.frequency: Continuous logging; retention enforced for minimum 7 years

requirements.requirement: Configure the GRC platform to maintain a registry mapping each licensed entity within the group to its applicable BNM supervisory department, with logic preventing submission workflows from being initiated without a validated department mapping in place. This registry must be version-controlled and updated whenever there is a regulatory classification change.
requirements.rationale: The 'as the case may be' language requires that the correct department is selected for each submission. A system-enforced registry prevents human error in department routing, which could result in a missed or misrouted submission — a direct compliance failure.
requirements.owner: Head of IT / Compliance Technology Lead
requirements.frequency: Registry reviewed and validated annually; updated immediately upon classification changes

governance.summary: Governance structures must establish clear accountability for the submission obligation under paragraph 18.2, including designated ownership at the senior management level, a formal internal policy governing the submission process, and board-level oversight of submission completion and outcomes. The governance framework must ensure the correct BNM department is identified per entity, that submissions are authorised at an appropriate level, and that any BNM feedback received post-submission is escalated and actioned systematically.

requirements.requirement: The Board or Board Risk Committee must formally mandate, via a board resolution or Terms of Reference, that the Chief Risk Officer (or equivalent senior officer) is accountable for ensuring the timely and complete submission of the self-assessment, gap analysis, and action plan to the appropriate BNM supervisory department under paragraph 18.2. This accountability must be documented in the relevant role's mandate or job description.
requirements.rationale: Without explicit senior ownership, submission may be treated as a lower-level administrative task, increasing the risk of delay, incomplete packaging, or incorrect routing. A board-level mandate elevates the obligation to a Senior Management accountability, consistent with the Standard ('S') marker's regulatory weight.
requirements.owner: Board Risk Committee Chairman / Board of Directors
requirements.frequency: Mandate established at policy inception; reviewed annually during Terms of Reference review

requirements.requirement: The institution must maintain a formal, board-approved Regulatory Submission Policy (or equivalent procedure document) that explicitly covers the requirements of paragraph 18.2, including: the identification of the applicable BNM supervisory department, the required submission contents (self-assessment, gap analysis, action plan), the internal approval hierarchy, submission deadlines, and the process for obtaining and retaining acknowledgement from BNM.
requirements.rationale: A documented policy ensures consistent execution of the submission obligation across reporting cycles and provides the governance baseline against which internal audit can assess compliance. The absence of a governing policy makes the submission process ad hoc and difficult to defend during BNM supervisory reviews.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed and approved annually; updated immediately upon changes to BNM guidance or internal structure

requirements.requirement: The institution must implement a formal Senior Management or EXCO-level sign-off process as a prerequisite for submission to BNM, requiring the CEO and CRO (at minimum) to formally endorse the completeness and accuracy of the submission package before it is dispatched. This endorsement must be documented and filed as part of the submission record.
requirements.rationale: The submission to BNM constitutes a formal regulatory representation by the institution. Senior management endorsement ensures accountability and prevents premature or inaccurate submissions, which could mislead the supervisory department and expose the institution to regulatory sanction.
requirements.owner: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Per-submission event

requirements.requirement: Internal Audit must include the paragraph 18.2 submission process within its annual audit plan, verifying that submissions were made to the correct BNM supervisory department, that all three required documents were included, that approvals were obtained at the appropriate level, and that acknowledgement records are retained. Findings must be reported to the Audit Committee.
requirements.rationale: Independent assurance over the submission process provides the Board with confidence that the paragraph 18.2 obligation was fulfilled correctly. It also identifies any process weaknesses before they result in regulatory findings during BNM's own supervisory review.
requirements.owner: Chief Internal Auditor / Head of Internal Audit
requirements.frequency: Annual audit coverage; findings reported to Audit Committee within 30 days of audit completion

requirements.requirement: The institution must establish a formal process for receiving, escalating, and responding to any feedback, queries, or directives issued by the relevant BNM supervisory department following receipt of the submission. This includes assigning an accountable owner for each BNM query, tracking responses to closure, and reporting the status of outstanding BNM engagements to the Board Risk Committee.
requirements.rationale: The submission under paragraph 18.2 initiates a supervisory dialogue. Without a structured response process, BNM queries may be mishandled, delayed, or inconsistently answered, which can escalate minor supervisory questions into formal findings or corrective action requirements.
requirements.owner: Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) / Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
requirements.frequency: Ongoing post-submission; status reported to Board Risk Committee at each quarterly meeting or upon material BNM communication
